name: Agilec (Admin/Corporate Office)

description:
I had a truly positive experience working with Chinmay at Agilec. From the very beginning, he took the time to understand my goals, strengths, and career aspirations. His professionalism, patience, and genuine care made a real difference in my job search journey.

Chinmay carefully updated my resume, provided clear and practical guidance throughout the process, and continuously encouraged me every step of the way. His support helped me build confidence and gain clarity about my career direction. What stood out most was his personalized approach—he truly wanted me to succeed and find the right opportunity, not just any job.

I am very grateful to Chinmay and the Agilec team for their support and dedication. I highly recommend his services to anyone looking for professional, compassionate, and results-driven career guidance.

Had a great experience and pleasure working with Mr.Chinmay Shah. An Amazing and cool consultant!
Chinmay's computer training workshop was very helpful and I gained lots of insights from it.
Highly recommend consulting with Mr.Shah for job related concerns.

I had a great experience working with Maseera at Agilec Courtice. She was extremely supportive, professional, and genuinely cared about helping me succeed. With her guidance and encouragement, I was able to secure a job opportunity. I truly appreciate her dedication and would highly recommend her services.

I had a great experience at Agilec Courtice, thanks to my caseworker, Zannat. She has been incredibly helpful, supportive, and professional throughout the entire process. She always took the time to listen, answer my questions, and guide me in the right direction. I’m truly grateful for her support and would highly recommend both Zannat and the Agilec team to anyone looking for career and employment assistance.

I attended Chinmay's job search workshop and computer classes at Agilec - Courtice location, and it was a game-changer! Chinmay's guidance on resume building, interview prep, and job search strategies was invaluable. The computer classes helped me brush up my skills and feel confident in my tech abilities. Chinmay's energy, expertise, and support made all the difference. Highly recommend for anyone looking to boost their job search and career prospects!"

name: Brampton South

description:
I had a great experience working with Preet as my employment consultant. She is professional, supportive, and genuinely cares about helping her clients succeed. She regularly checked in with me about my job search and made sure I knew about available resources and supports, including employment incentives and work clothing assistance.

Preet is very responsive and easy to communicate with. She takes the time to explain everything clearly and makes you feel supported throughout the process. I truly appreciate her guidance and encouragement.

Highly recommend her services to anyone looking for employment support!

Preet was extremely helpful during my job search. She guided me through the process and informed me about job fairs and new opportunities. Her support and timely communication made a big difference. I truly appreciate her help and highly recommend her!

Today I went with my husband for appointment with preet mam , so we both had a great experience with her she’s polite explained everything very well. And I also want to thanks Christina mam to attend us so nicely. Overall it was great experience.

I had a wonderful experience with Achieve Consultant Preet Soor. She consistently managed monthly and quarterly follow-ups to stay updated on my progress and provided valuable support throughout. Preet guided me in accessing benefits, assisted with job search direction, kept me informed about job fairs, and offered helpful advice whenever needed.
Thank you, Preet, for your dedicated support, it is truly appreciated.

My name is Basil Chinenye. I have completed my computer classes with Saafi Faynus on digital Literacy. Saafi is a wonderful Teacher who understands the subject matter of what she teaches. She's so focused and desirous in making sure her class understands what she teacher. I can say that I have benefitted from topics like , digital AI, Google etc. I am happy to have participated in this class.

name: Mississauga City Centre
address: 50 Burnhamthorpe Road West, Suite 300, Mississauga, ON L5B 3C2, Canada
description:
Achēv is a solid resource, especially if you’re newer to the Canadian job market or rebuilding momentum. I primarily worked with Grace, and she’s the real deal — professional, well-prepared, and genuinely in your corner. Her interview prep is sharp, and that matters because job hunting right now is hard. I always left feeling like someone was actually rooting for me. Connected with some good people through the workshops too. That last part is underrated.

Worth knowing going in: they have job developers and employee liaisons, but they’re not a placement agency in the traditional sense. If you’re a senior professional in a specialized field, the opportunities may not match your level. Not a knock — just context. For what they do and who they primarily serve, they do it well.

I would like to formally express my sincere appreciation to Achēv for the professional and effective support I received from the very first time I visited their office. They have assisted me with many matters, including my academic credential evaluation.
I would also like to extend my particular gratitude to Ms. Zinah Abdullah for her exemplary assistance. She was extremely helpful, patient, and supportive, and her thoughtful guidance throughout the evaluation process was greatly appreciated.

I have been attending Achēv for a while now and their services are consistently making my interviewing and job hunting skills better a bit at a time. I would also like to thank Dahlia Habtom and Grace for their workshop on interviewing skills today as it helped me gain comprehensive insight on how interviewers think and act. PS I had a sudden interview request yesterday that is due tomorrow and Dahlia decided to go the extra mile and booked me for a session at the end of her working hours to conduct a mock interview with me to help me overcome my interview anxiety, So thank you Dahlia there is not enough words to express how grateful I am to you.

Working with my career specialist at Achēv Mississauga was a very positive experience. She provided valuable guidance, practical advice, and consistent encouragement throughout my career journey. Her dedication and professionalism made me feel supported every step of the way. I truly appreciate her help and would highly recommend her to anyone looking to grow and advance in their career.

Attending the ACHĒV Mississauga Employment Services for Youth program was an amazing experience.
Althea and Osama guided us with patience, support, and clear direction throughout the three weeks of in-person training.
They treated everyone equally and made sure each person felt valued and encouraged.
As a newcomer from India, I often felt lost, but they helped me gain confidence and understand where to start.
With their mentorship, I was able to secure a job as a Pharmacy Assistant, which was my dream role.
I am truly grateful to them for showing me the right path during a very difficult time.
I strongly recommend this program to any youth looking for genuine guidance and career support.

name: Karis Disability Services — East District Office Programs

Sad times overshadowed my time with some VERY amazing individuals.

Great day connecting with the team and learning about the great service and support towards building communities of belonging for all.

name: YWCA Hamilton Opportunities Program
address: YWCA MacNab Main Location
description:
The YWCA is a wonderful place that helps women. But I do think they need to re train their male security guards when it comes to boundaries with women and personal space. It is not appropriate for male guards to hover inches behind vulnerable women without their knowledge or consent. It can be extremely triggering for somone who has been abused. Personal space matters. Respect for boundaries matters.

I especially love this place because it is dedicated to serving women and the elderly, great! I fell in love with the RANA coaching staff since last Friday, and there is an hour-long free course this Friday at 12:00～1:00time

The supervised access center is very disorganized and is very hard to get into contact with. I believe that Natalie is in charge of the supervised access center and she never answers her phone. There has been numerous times that I have had to have the YCWA receptionist send emails to her just to be called back by a “No Caller ID” that I mistaken for spam. I am in court to get visitation with my child and the judge granted me approval to get supervised access, which is supposed to upgrade to unsupervised access after a certain number of visits. It’s been 6+ months and I haven’t been able to get 1 visit done because I cannot reach her. They haven’t even completed my ex wife’s registration yet and I’ve already payed for both of our registration fees! Ive spent over $13,000 dollars in court/lawyer fees because we keep having to make new settlement conference dates because I am not meeting my visitation requirements due to their lack of care.

A few years ago I started going there, and was the best experience ever. Equipment is ok but the workers that were there were the top notch. I went there not only to lose weight but it actually cleared my mental mind. It was an oasis for me. I loved every minute of it. Any of the staff were there to help you at all times. Some of the staff were knowledgeable in mental illness. They were trained well because the ones I spoke with actually helped me. It absolutely sucks that this place is not a safe place I  go to anymore. They closed  . Most of all it kept my mind clear, better than any medication I've been on in my 40 something years. I am so upset and devastated that this place is not open for us anymore. My life is destroyed again but if you guys open up again that would be the best thing to ever happen to me and I'm sure so many other woman an hour community.

Floor staff are great, in gym and pool, good people.

Policies and service from "the office" leave much to be desired. Also they seem to clean house on support staff for the shelter every other year, and im not confident in their priorities regarding the use of funding.

description:
PATH Employment Services offers genuinely caring and personalized support for job seekers. The staff are knowledgeable, encouraging, and truly invested in helping people find meaningful work. A great resource for anyone looking for guidance and confidence in their job search. ~Dm.

So I worked with Meagan at path to help find employment and I have to say this place is top notch. The staff are wonderful and Meagan guided through resume building, sites to use to find jobs (not indeed :p) and generally just had an amazing demeanor and willingness to help. I seriously recommend this place to anyone struggling to find employment in the current job market. If I could give this place more than five stars I would.

Brandon

It’s been a very pleasing experience with Minoo and Nicole who has been very supportive to my job search and support. I got a job within two months of registering with them (even though it’s underemployment). They’ve been really helpful and ready to help at any given opportunity!

I am very grateful to work with SANAZ, she's truly professional, kind and caring person.With her support and guidance I feel very motivated.
All staff are amazing and I am very grateful that they support me.
Thank you SANAZ,and thanks to all nice peoples.
Happy Thanksgiving Day!!
❣️❣️

I am very grateful to have worked with Minoo. She is caring, kind, and truly professional. She found several great job opportunities for me, and I even received multiple offers. With her support and guidance, I was able to choose the best one. I highly recommend Minoo for anyone looking for dedicated and knowledgeable support in their job search.

name: VPI Employment Services (Hamilton Location)
address: VPI Employment Services Hamilton Location, Hamilton, ON, L9C 3A6
description:
Life changed for me recently in a big way and VPI employment services has been a huge relief to me. My counselor Fathima has been working closely with me to help me develop my skills, get my confidence back and to help me be job ready. This past week both Fathima and Mishal have been running a workshop called Power of Possible for clients like myself and it has been extremely helpful! Just some of the things I've been learning is how to identify my skills, being prepared for tough interview questions, strategies on managing money and how to be assertive. VPI offers a lot of tools and resources to help build your confidence and get you ready to be apart of Canada's work force. Whether if you're new to Canada or just have been out of the job market for a while, they can offer you something to help you get on your career path. It's also great to know that once you get a job they still work with you to make sure you have the tools to be successful. I wasn't aware of such places like VPI prior to my life changes. I wish I had or I could of made some changes sooner! I'm excited to see what my future has in store thanks to them!

I want to express my deepest gratitude to Abby, who accompanied me for several months during my job search. She guided me with patience and dedication on how to improve my resume and all the details needed to secure a position.
I also want to sincerely thank Mishal, who with an excellent attitude supported me in obtaining a voucher to buy the clothing and essentials I needed to start my new job.
Thank you to VPI Hamilton, because right now many of us truly need support to begin working. The help you provide to purchase essentials, clothing, shoes, boots, etc, is invaluable.
A thousand thanks, and may you continue with this excellent work that so many people need.

I am very thankful to VPI. They support me well.

Specifically i would like to say thanks n pay salute to my Counselor Fathima Sincy, she is very humble,  supportive n kind hearted lady. She listens me with great patience n give the best solutions.
May Almighty God bless her n her family too Amen🙏.

This review is based on my cousin’s experience with VPI Employment Services, and it was terrible from start to finish. The receptionist was consistently rude and unprofessional, which made every visit stressful. There was no sense of welcome or willingness to help.

My cousin worked with Atta and his manager, and neither provided any real support. They didn’t follow up, didn’t offer useful guidance, and acted like my cousin was only there to help them meet their numbers. It genuinely felt like their priority was securing government funding, not helping people find jobs.

On top of that, once they open a file, they make it extremely difficult to close it or transfer to another employment centre. It traps people in a system that isn’t helping them at all.

Based on my cousin’s experience, I would strongly recommend avoiding this place and finding an employment service that actually cares about the people it’s supposed to support.

From day one, I was promised help, but after the initial conversation they stopped communicating and provided absolutely nothing. For three months, I received zero real support. I found a job entirely on my own, which makes their involvement pointless.

Only after I secured work without them did they suddenly start asking for my personal information, with no clear explanation. Total disorganization.

The entire experience felt chaotic, misleading, and incompetent. There was no follow-through, no accountability, and no respect for my time.

I asked them to close my file and clearly stated that they are not authorized to use or share my personal information, especially information connected to Work Anywhere. I will not deal with this company again.

I strongly discourage anyone from using this service.

name: Agilec (Hamilton Office)
address: Agilec Hamilton Office, Hamilton, ON, L8S 1G5
description:
My experience with Agilec has been the best I've ever had, and a huge part of that is thanks to Chanelle Di Marco. From day one, she demonstrated incredible empathy and professionalism, guiding me with clear tools and unwavering support.

It is rare to find a place where you feel so genuinely heard and valued. Thanks to the dedication of Chanelle and the whole Agilec team, I feel much more confident in my career path. No wonder why they hold the Gold Status recognition from Excellence Canada. If you are looking for real help for your future, this is the place to go to.

/Robert Guevara

I am deeply grateful to Agilec for the outstanding job workshop and the exceptional support they provided throughout my job-seeking journey. The workshop was well-structured, practical, and empowering, offering clear guidance on resume building, interview preparation, effective job search strategies, and a strong understanding of the Canadian job market.

I would like to give special recognition to Shweta Bhatia, my dedicated support worker, who supported me consistently from beginning to end. Her patience, professionalism, encouragement, and genuine care made a significant difference in my experience. She took the time to understand my goals, provided personalized guidance, and remained supportive and responsive throughout the entire process. Her commitment truly went above and beyond.

Through Agilec’s guidance—and Shweta’s unwavering support—I gained not only practical tools but also renewed confidence, clarity, and motivation in pursuing meaningful employment. Their encouragement strengthened my belief in my abilities and helped me move forward with purpose.

I highly recommend Agilec to anyone seeking employment support, career development, or job-readiness training. They are doing remarkable work by empowering individuals to rebuild confidence and successfully transition into new opportunities.

Shweta has been an absolute best and very supportive throughout the entire process. She has helped me with interview preparation, documentation and logistics, and is always responsive and quick. I really appreciate the support in helping me find secure and meaningful employment, thank you Shweta!

If you ever have a job, and need help trying to get things or transportation for your job this is the place to go! Omar is hands down one of the most polite, patient and helpful people I’ve ever met. Every time I need help or have an issue or need something fixed to get ready for employment, even at Short notice, he always comes through and make sure I have what I need. He checks in with me to see how I’m doing and shows that he truly cares about me as a person! Because of Omar, I had found employment and I am happy working and I couldn’t have done it without this man standing behind me with this support and the help with this company and I recommend if you come to this company try to be as lucky as I was and ask to have Omar because having him on your team means success! Thank you Omar for everything I wouldn’t be where I’m standing today with out all your help! Thank you

Shweta and Agilec as a whole have been very professional and supportive. Shweta has gone above and beyond to support me and others, sharing expert advice, checking in regularly and being incredibly encouraging. The guidance received from her is exemplary and one that many others would benefit greatly from.

name: CHOICES Location (CHOICES Association Inc. Services)
address: Dundas, ON, L9H 5E2
description:
While the agency employs many very good front line workers, the management does not respect clients and their families, and knowingly pays abusers a monthly per diem to provide housing to clients.
They require their employees and contracted host families to complete abuse training annually, while the agency is completely unable to address abuse claims when they are reported. They use the ministry as a shield and weapon against any challenges. The agency is a disgrace and the Province of Ontario needs to investigate them and close them down before more clients are abused.

Gotta be something we can do to help make these homes better. Slamming staff. Firing staff. Make more rules. Pass more legislation. Doesn't seem to be working to improve housing and care in group homes for the most vulnerable people. I have been to Choices on many occasions and for my brief visit things seem okay. Then I read reviews like these here.

VERY RUDE!! We tried to park for a few mins in a thunderstorm and we told them it’s unsafe to drive yet they still kicked us out even though the parking lot was empty!!! Disappointing

Dedicated staff who work hard to deliver person-centered care.

Our organization purchases products here. Great staff

name: Community Living Burlington - Integrated Employment Services (Burlington - Mainway)
address: Burlington, ON, L7M 1A1
description:
Disappointed with the management with the people involved with the group homes for CLO in Burlington.  The two young ladies that are on my bowling team were stranded by their groups homes.  I was informed by their manager that they did not have a ride home that evening after our bowling session was completed.  Not only did they inform at the end of the night but they had no solution as to how these girls would get home!  The group home worker informed me that she reached out to her management and other collaborating group homes and they had absolutely NO ONE to pickup these girls nor other alternatives for them to come home.  All around bad management and a terrible lack of responsibility for the safety of these two young women.  As their coach I will be writing to CLO outlining the poor management I have encountered this year with their two groups homes with CLO in Burlington!  Special Needs 5 Pin Olympic bowling.

Don’t send your child here! Will randomly cancel programs and leave you high and dry. No conversations, no care at all about your child and their development. I wish we have never discovered them and sent our child there. AVOID, AVOID, AVOID.

It's a good place where residents thrive positively.
I love the fact that training and retraining programs are always available for new and old support staff members.

Wonderful venue to volunteer some time and be useful.

Great organisation delivering top quality service

name: Ontario Disability Support Program (Ontario Ministry of Children, Community and Social Services)
address: Itabashi Way, Burlington, ON L7M 5A5
description:
Some workers seriously need proper training as clearly they do not understand anything about the Odsp. Never returns calls,sends you on wild goose chases and false hope,makes your mental health worse than ever etc. Except for Thea V. One of the best staff members there ever. Love her as she is so helpful and very supportive and understanding. Thank you T.

No one ever answers the phone...the extension I was provided doesn't work... It would be nice to be able to get in touch with my case worker. Does anyone even work reception there?

On Friday, March 7th, I had an urgent issue because ODSP Brampton had messed up my documents and set an unreasonably short deadline. When I called Burlington ODSP, a gentleman answered immediately and was incredibly helpful. I explained my situation, and without hesitation, he offered to print the necessary documents for me. Within 15 minutes, I arrived at the office, and everything was ready for me to sign—saving me the stress of waiting for the mail and missing the deadline.

Both he and a lady at the office were extremely polite, professional, and efficient. Their kindness and quick action made a huge difference for me. I’m truly grateful for their outstanding service!

Thank you to case manager Janine. Had issue with my odsp payment & called office to enquire.  Was told my case manager had switched to Janine who I left message. Turns out she not my new case manager; yet she promptly responded & assured me payment was ok, told me when to expect payment & who in fact is my new case worker. Thank you very much Janine.

No one answers the phone despite being on hold for almost 1 hour.

I am beyond grateful for the support I received from this agency. They helped me navigate and take care of funding for my nursing career at a time when I truly needed it.

Noella, in particular, is incredibly kind, patient, and compassionate. She went out of her way to help me, took the time to explain everything clearly, and never made me feel rushed or judged. She genuinely gave me hope and reminded me that there are good people out there who truly care.

As a nursing student, financial stress can feel overwhelming, and the support I received made a real difference in my life. I cannot thank Noella and the team enough for their dedication and kindness. I would highly recommend this agency to anyone who needs guidance and support.

I was in the Employment Help Centre Grimsby and found the service outstanding and highly recommended going in to see Rosie and Eileen if looking for any kind of help!!!@

name: Manpower
address: Ottawa, ON, K1P 5H2
description:
I had a great experience with Manpower! The recruitment process was smooth and efficient, and the team was very professional and supportive. They matched me with a job quickly, ensuring that it aligned with my skills and availability. Communication was clear, and they provided all the necessary details to help me succeed in my role. I highly recommend Manpower to anyone looking for reliable employment opportunities. Thank you for the great service!

Manpower is a reputable job agency, they really understand how the market works and provide all the necessary support you need, their staff are also awesome. I have recommended friends to the agency and will continue recommending. Great job 👍

This is a scam. They send endless, non-stop, unsolicited messages. The initial message describes a “job opportunity” with a pay scale. When you tell them you are not interested they miraculously can make the pay scale greater to seem more appealing

A very healthy and professional company to work with!  I enjoyed the family like atmosphere and accommodations the Manpower Veritaaq team provided to support my work from home position.   On my in-office days, I found the atmosphere, cozy lunch room and work stations  quite comfortable with lots of beautiful windows. Free coffee lattes were a definite perk!  My recruiter was Brigette Lariviere who went the distance to professionally provide all I needed to ensure an easy start to my employment.  I recommend Brigette and Manpower Veritaaq if you are searching for healthy employment services or a wonderful team to work with.

My experience with Karina McAuliffe from Manpower Ottawa was seamless and great. Thank you for your help Karina.

name: WCG International Consultants (Integrated Employment Services, Ottawa)
address: Ottawa, ON, K2P 1L4
description:
WCG Employment Services played a big role in helping me secure a job. I’m especially grateful to Khushboo Nangru for her support throughout the process. She helped me improve my resume, guided me in targeting the right opportunities in my field, and provided valuable advice during my job search. Her guidance made the whole process much smoother and more focused. I also want to thank Simran Kohli for her support and assistance. I truly appreciate the team’s efforts and would highly recommend WCG to anyone looking for employment services.

Thanks and heads up for the khushboo,  my  employment consultant  at WCG employment service Brampton.
She demonstrated exceptional expertise, professionalism, and dedication.
Her guidance was clear,thorough and her genuine care for her clients truly set her apart. I felt supported and confident every step of the way.
Khushboo is an outstanding consultant and anyone fortunate enough to work with her will benefit immensely from her knowledge and commitment.
ONCE AGAIN THANKS TO HER TREMENDOUS WORK.💯💯💯💯🙏🙏🙏

Misty at WCG Brampton is excellent! She is professional, attentive, and genuinely cares about helping her clients succeed.
She has been incredibly helpful with my employment needs and is very responsive to my requests.
If you need employment assistance, definitely ask for Misty.

Misty  has a very kind and respectful personality.
She is very detail minded and a good listener
I am happy to have been assigned to her as my
Career Consultant.
I am very grateful for all her support and look forward for her advice and moral support.

I wish her more success in her line of work

Navigating a career change can be stressful, but Misty at WCG Employment Services made it feel manageable. Her support was personalized and professional, giving me the tools and confidence I needed to succeed. I’m now happily settled in my new position. If you need efficient assistance in your job search, WCG Employment Services is your best option!

name: Performance Plus Rehabilitative Care Inc
address: Ottawa, ON K2P 1L4
description:
They did not help me feel ready for a job and their other assistance felt like they wasted my time. I regret signing up with them despite having paid nothing to do so.

Performance Plus Rehabilitative Care Inc is hidden gem with great course to expand skills to develop towards a workplace.

I believe being patience with each team member is very important as they all instruments as team leading, directing, guiding each client to serve each one as best as they can with the tools they are given to provide each client.

I will keep expanding my skills with this company as I am very happy with what I have learned so far; I have moments with an consultant I choose to be direct and firm to connect with the team to receive what I am seeking to achieve the career goals.

I wish Performance Plus Rehabilitative Care Inc will receive renewed positive feedback in the future because I see the good, the potential, And I see area of improvement in this service for  who all seek this program to build the skills toward resilience for change for the business, the employees, and every client because each person is working hard towards their goals to achieve in success in many ways.

God bless.

I can't express enough gratitude for the incredible support I received from Performance Plus Rehabilitation Care Inc. (PPRC). They not only played a pivotal role in helping me to retain employment, but also went above and beyond by offering unwavering support, personalized skill training and constant encouragement. Their commitment to empowering individuals with disabilities extends beyond job placement - they have helped me to learn how to advocate for my rights including the right for reasonable accommodation so that I can sustainably participate in the workforce. Being able to participate in the workforce will directly lead to an increase in security and stability for my young family. They also taught me how to navigate the professional landscape with optimism, focus and confidence. I'm immensely thankful for their dedicated team and the positive impact they make. I would highly recommend this organization for their transformative approach, authenticity and genuine care!

It took some time to get my first appointment, but it was totally worth it. They helped me revamp my cv, create a solid cover letter, and find/apply for jobs. They helped me get a good job in the end. It wasn't instant, it depends what field your interested in. I got several different case workers over my time there and some were better than others, but most were very good. Don't expect to get a job on the first day and it can take several months to get the right job for you. That's just the nature of the job market. As I am sure you noticed, it is very hard to find good work these days. It can takes months before organizations even reply to you with an interview invite, and many organizations won't reply at all. You just have to be persistent. That's the reality of the modern job market. I stayed with them after I got a job with their help and eventually they connected me with a job in my field of interest.

Written Dec 2023

In Feb/April 2023 I came to realize that I was on the autism spectrum. And in May I began working with PPRC. What followed was a mix of incompetence, indifference and utter lack of empathy that has left me worse off than when I began.

Some notable issues include:
- An excessively long process to get to Job Development. Which after researching other providers, I have learned is atypical.
- Resumes that were incorrectly formatted or had spelling errors.
- No support from my job developer. Either in the form of leads or follow-ups
- Emails that were not replied to. Vital questions that were never answered.

Those are just the top-line examples I have many others. What made the experience so awful, was that I assumed PPRC and its management cared about helping disabled people find sustainable work. In all my time dealing with them I felt like a bother, an annoyance and that they did not care, whatsoever, about helping me or my situation.

I have no doubt, whatsoever that had I either worked with a different provider or on my own I would not be in the current situation I find myself in.

Avoid PPRC and find another provider. They are out there.

Now I’m studying in the new English class which is so perfect for me. I’m really appreciate for her help!

I was successfully removed from Toronto school and now wait for OCCSC's class. I'm so happy there's a Chinese service centre in Ottawa. Thank you so much!

Excellent experience, friendly and helpful staff, comfortable testing room, visitors waiting room and well maintained restrooms.

wanted to learn more about how to enrol my spouse into the LINC english lessons that they offer, called about 14 times in the last week and a half on various times from Monday to Friday only getting ahold of 1 person whom told me to contact her colleague via dialing a different extension. Left around 2-3 voice messages and have never heard back from anyone so for now giving a 1 star...

name: Belong Ottawa - Centre 454
address: Ottawa, ON K1N 7M8
description:
Everybody is welcome the staff are so good its a drop-in where you have breakfast and lunch from monday till Sunday and have access  for laundry shower clothing, they help with ODSP and OW application and also you can sleep from 8am till 2pm its worth to visit awesome staff and great manager.

I was in active addiction and homeless in Ottawa from 2017-2020..I used the 454 many times for a shower and wash my clothes have a bite to eat and geta drink and keep warm..I'm so grateful for all the help you provided..Well I'm housed and have been clean since September 15th/2020...WE DO RECOVER...THANK YOU SO MUCH

It's like a second home and the staff is great I couldn't imagine my day without it

Nobody understands just how vigilant and point these guys are, to the extent they literally save lives.  Some people have nowhere to go, That struggle with addiction I literally witnessed them save lives on more than one occasion.  Absolute superhero’s come to work every day that don’t wear capes.

Genuinely these warriors do not get the credit they deserve; on top
Of wanting to return to this type of environment every day, deserve a daily thank you for what they do.  Even if they don’t do anything, they’re still here for anyone that needs any type of support.

I cannot thank you guys enough for your daily grind against lifelong difficulties some people are forced to endure.

Each and every single one of you deserve medals of honor.

My voice may not be worth much, but it’s worth something…

I cannot thank you guys enough..  truly,

Thank you, for everything you guys do.   From social assistance getting city support, to literally and genuinely saving lives….

I also have to throw out a big time 🤜🏼🤛🏼 to a proper piece to this arsenal, otherwise known as    Havv Mercy.   I need to publicly thank this guy being a final piece to this arsenal operating here.

Having a directly positive impact to us on a moral level, that I had to personally thank this man beca….

This guy is working 12 hour shifts on rotation crazy hour ranges.   His one day off, he comes and starts trimming and cutting hair for everyone.

This dude takes his time and resulted no shitting the best haircut I’ve ever had.

Moral boost big time!!

Mad respect to everyone that donates any time here….

I’m not always comfortable with situations, but from what’s around;
I feel the most….
Comfortable here.
💯🤜🏼🤛🏼

Thank you, each and every one of you.

I love the 454 I'm always able to clean my laundry  take shower in a clean environment, able to eat breakfast and drink plenty of Tea ... I thank God for all of this..

Nice gym. Cheaper membership than most other places around. Also offers basketball and swimming.

I have never tried anywhere else and I don't think I will try anywhere else as long as this beautiful place exists.
I am surprised by the negative comments because everything here is wonderful.

name: Ontario Disability Support Program - Employment Supports
address: 351 Preston St
description:
I went today at 10:30 in the morning and the lady at the reception was uncooperative, very defensive, and instead of offering help, she kept repeating the same phrases (which were completely unhelpful to me). Despite my asking her to help me and explaining that I was not there to bother her or anyone else, she was hostile and her approach lacked any professionalism.It was a very bad experience. I hope this establishment will choose a more professional and friendly reception staff.

Unfortunately it is an absolute shame that the service provided by the receptionists at the center is one of the worst experiences I could ever have. I came in with my dad to discuss something we were confused about with clear errors in calculations made by ODSP and one of the receptionist was awfully rude to my dad and asserting that it was my dads fault and completely lacked any care, compassion or at the very least professionalism. You’d think that for a job that requires you to work in client service in a sensitive environment and working with individuals with disabilities the staff would have more compassion when speaking with clients. It is a shame on the whole organization that you hire individuals like this. There needs to be some serious client service retraining.

Funnily enough the lady asked my dad in a rude and condescending way if he read the conditions before applying to ODSP which was around 2006; however, the lady clearly hasn’t read the work contract she signed or any of the training she surely received (I hope) on dealing with vulnerable individuals in this job and how to handle client cases with sensitivity. Pretty ridiculous if you ask me. She went on to tell her colleagues her side of the story so they also met us with an attitude after a unfortunate encounter with her but we managed to deal better with another lady who seemed unwilling to listen the entire time.

What is happening. My medical supply was cut off in September because nobody sent me a form. My worker is Samia who never returns calls. Her supervisor doesn't seem to know how to return calls either. Nobody works there except reception and they can't help you.

I needed to drop off a large cheque earlier today, because I arrived before 5 pm, but unfortunately I was denied access to the building, because a security guard told me that this building closes at 4:50 pm.

It's better than nothing while fighting for your life and your family especially your little ones .Thank you very much for the little support from  you even though ain't enough but God knows we do appreciate odsp every day. God save ontario free ontario.

description:

Friendly place with professional receptionists. The first floor is for family/kids' stuff and the second floor you can have resources for job search. There you'll find a nice spot with computers and a printer (it is not time-limited)

Apparently the air conditioning goes out regularly. Makes me happy I don't have to work there or have to use that building on any regular basis. A Dream Come True Thanks to Kakomire Kashongwe

I received my Permanent Residency thanks to the incredible efforts of Kakomire Kashongwe. Before finding Kakomire Kashongwe, I faced numerous challenges with my PR application. However, from our first meeting, I knew I was in good hands.

Kakomire Kashongwe was not only professional and knowledgeable but also incredibly dedicated. He meticulously reviewed every detail of my case, provided clear guidance, and was always available to answer my questions. One instance that stood out was when he worked hard to ensure my application was submitted on time.

Thanks to Kakomire Kashongwe's hard work, my application was approved, and I can now call Canada my home. This has been a life-changing experience for me, and my children I am forever grateful.

I highly recommend Kakomire Kashongwe to anyone seeking immigration assistance. His hard work and dedication truly made my dream come true.

name: Career Education, Careers & Experience
address: University Community Centre, Room 210, 1151 Richmond St, N6A 3K7
description:
UCC contains a lot of shops for snacks and drinking. Those wishing for a relaxation after class may be interested in this place. It also has the bookstore, glasses store, and some other stores. Also, it has a lot of classrooms and I once had some math classes there. It is worth going and seeing.

The spoke, the wave upstairs, and the Theos coffee machine in the food hub area are some of my favorite spots for coffee around campus! The place is always busy, so be ready to stand in line for a while if you come by for food or drinks around noon. Lots of seating for studying or hanging out, though not many quiet spots. The book store downstairs of course has all your course texts and school merch, but the fun books and stationary all have a good selection too. A great campus hub for student activities! 🎒

We come to see movies here.  We have for years and love the popcorn!  It is reasonable priced.  The screen is great and seating comfortable.  It's not a recliner but does the job nicely.  Always has great movies and love that they return sometimes after a first run.

Great, affordable on campus dining, featuring a well rounded menu. Items include sandwiches, burgers, salads, rice bowls and constantly changing features menus!

Lots of food options and seating

Kritika took the time to listen, understand my goals, and provide guidance that was both practical and motivating. Thanks to her support, I felt more confident about my next steps and better prepared for new opportunities.

Highly recommended

If my worker isn't available for a scheduled meet, or I have been signed up for a cancelled event, I do not find out until I have wasted my time and gas that it has been cancelled.  Not only is the process extremely stressful I am feeling that I have been pushed to the back burner because of my age and physical abilities.

Everybody in employment north is absolutely amazing. They have helped me since iv been up north for the past year. They all have amazing skills and really help when you need it ! Ashley, Duke, Brenda,Debbie all of them are very skilled to help you with what you are looking for. I highly recommend this place for anyone looking for a job or anyone who is looking for a new career. Thank you folks you all have been amazing.

ISSUE RESOLVED

Staff is very eager to help and answer questions and offer information. Well organized and stocked with valuable resources for all kinds of services from employment to volunteering and community events and programs.

Great new office. Experienced staff looking to help people in the community.

name: Employment North — Gravenhurst Employment Office
address: 2-410 Muskoka Road South, Gravenhurst, ON P1P 1J4
description:
Super impressed by the quality of service. I spoke with Site Coordinator this morning (Janet) and she gave me all the information I needed to know to understand the Canada-Ontario Job Grant process. She was so supportive in helping me access all of the supports available to my business. Such a fantastic resource here in Muskoka for business leaders and owners. Thank you, Janet and team for being great at your job and clearly loving what you do! It shows!

I had just moved back to the area and employment north assisted in helping with expenses. Which was very helpful and very easy to work with them. Id recommend that if you are trying to get resettled then be sure to talk with them and ask about how they can help you. Thank you employment north. God Bless - I never give a perfect score on anything as I believe there is no definition of perfect. We are always getting better.

Update; They are definately the goto people for local work. Im still super impressed! Excellent people and fantastic resources. They have a caring staff and really have a knack to get anyone back on track for work. From a complete makeover of your resume and cover letter to coaching and sourcing real work. No matter what our circumstances are.  In short, if you are unemployed or under employed, this is the place to go. Also they don't make you feel uncomfortable.  And they don't judge.  They have computers for use as well and have specific training programs to get us onwards and upwards.  Also, occasionally, they have Barbeques and cook a perfect hotdog and serve apple pie. That is the way a community outreach service should be run. I'm super impressed with John and all the team there. They are very well connected in the community, I was pleasantly surprised at the in-depth research they do around my job search.
New Update: make sure you ask about the assustance if you want to change careers.

Alexis is a great first impression of your office. She was very courteous and helpful and really made for a nice visit.

Employment North (Gravenhurst) was very helpful and welcoming. Sydney was especially helpful and pointed me in the right direction. Very knowledgeable and offers great advice. Thank you Sydney for a great experience at Employment North.

name: Accessible Employment Services
address: 520 Sutherland Drive, Toronto, Ontario M4G 3V9
description:
Great rehabilitation site for the ones in need. The staff is very accommodating and very professional towards all the patients.

If you're in need of rehabilitation please try this site you will not be disappointed and it's just very close to Sunnybrook hospital which is located on Bayview and Lawrence so you're not too far away great place to go to.

Very impressed what they did for my mother and much appreciated to all the team.
Great job!

Received great care and compassion here. They taught me to walk again and what to expect in my “new” life living with a SCI. Roommates became friends for life.

Our daughter has spent 100+ days and the progress after spinal surgery at SunnyBrook has been amazing. The doctors are on top of everything and the care team assigned was very good. But most of all we want to point out the exceptional work of Holly (OT) and Aaron (PT). Their care, compassion and encouragement helped her to push through many valleys along the way.
We thanked them in person this week but wanted to publicly do so on here.
Paul and Carol

Our daughter has been at Lyndhurst over a hundred days and will be leaving this week and while that’s exciting we can’t believe the exceptional care she has had. We are so impressed with the team and the progress is unbelievable, not just for our daughter but all who are there. Aaron (pt)  and Holly (ot) are so good at what they do and so dedicated to their patients.
The drs are on top of everything and the nurses and PSWs too, everyone is friendly and the equipment amazing
Thank you lyndhurst staff for doing what you do.

Amazing staff and equipment.  My physiotherapist was Sofia and I could not have been more impressed with the professionalism amd knowledge.  All the nursing staff are amazing and helpful.  Some of the night staff can be overwhelmed/subpar but overall very impressive.  One advice would be to talk to other patients and staff and constantly inquire about treatment options available.  People spend weeks or months without knowing about or benefiting from: the therapeutic pool, electro stimulation therapy, parafin wax therapy, bikes that move paralized legs for cramp management, stimulation and joint maintenance etc.

name: Achēv

description:

Parveen possesses a comprehensive understanding of the rigorous requirements and operational nuances inherent in airport-based positions. She provided strategic guidance that went well beyond standard recruitment, offering meticulous preparation that ensured my candidacy was aligned with the highest industry standards.
I credit Parveen’s distinguished industry insight and steadfast commitment as the primary factors in my successful placement. Her ability to navigate complex hiring landscapes with professionalism and efficiency is truly exemplary. I really appreciate her from bottom of my heart.

I cannot thank Krystyna Zamko enough for her help. What stood out the most was her genuine enthusiasm and how much she clearly cared about my progress. She was a huge part of helping me find a career path I actually enjoy. Thanks to her support, I’m now in trade school for plumbing, and it’s easily the best decision I’ve ever made. If you’re looking for a caseworker who is friendly, supportive, and truly wants to see you succeed, Krystyna is the best.

I had a great experience with Nuzhat Parveen at Achēv Malton . She helped me a lot in finding a job and supported me throughout the entire process. She is very kind, professional, and always ready to help. She genuinely cares about people and gives her best effort. I highly recommend her services. She is an amazing person to work with.

**Achev - Mississauga Malton Review**

I recently visited the Achev office located in  7420 Airport Rd, Malton and had a positive experience. The facility is clean, organized, and professional, creating a welcoming atmosphere right from the start. The staff was friendly and attentive, offering assistance with a variety of employment and settlement services. They seem genuinely committed to helping newcomers and job seekers navigate the system, offering practical advice and resources.

I particularly appreciated their personalized approach whether you're looking for career counseling, training programs, or support with job applications, they take the time to understand your unique needs. The workshops they offer are informative, and their team is knowledgeable about the current job market and opportunities.

Overall, Achev is a great community resource, especially for anyone looking to advance their career or settle into the area. I would definitely recommend their services to others.

Everyone were so supportive and the service was excellent thank you

Nice a

name: Canadian Council on Rehabilitation and Work
address: 1867 Yonge Street, Suite 906, Toronto, Ontario M4S 1Y5
description:
Anajali has no communication skills. Very untransparent, ignorant, & unprofessional. Now I understand why they have such high turnover, not many clients. This organization is a joke. Don't waste your time with them as they won't help you find a job. Tired of their attitude.

Unresponsive, unhelpful, no transparency or clear communication. They pretend to help you find jobs but have very little success.

I get pay by them for training a job. They don't know about person up and down behavior. I blew this Job training youth the future. I got booted out youth the future but about offensive things about aggressive family problem. They are nice facilitator employees. That Youth the Future manager booted out because my comments. I like about Stephen Irish because he good man helping people get a job. I shouldn't illegally at Youth the Future at webcam. It cost so much stress at home at online video camera. It a good program.

I have been trying to find a job. I heard about this agency but was told that college degree was a must. I forced myself to go to the college and amazing I made it. I finally got in but they did not do anything to find me a job. I left and found better agency. Not recommended! Their policy is very silly "2 years per client" while disability still facing barriers. Thumbs down!

They stole 2 weeks from life with their useless employment program.They don't answer most of your questions about their services and what they can do for you in terms of employment and disability until the end of the program. They keep saying save your question for the end. At the end, you find out that they practically do nothing.No employer is stupid enough to refuse to hire or cease anybody`s employment because of their disability, so that people need you advocate for them, nor your help for the disclosure of the disability .Don`t waste your time, you can learn more on the internet in couple hours rather than their entire program.

name: Canadian Hearing Services
address: 271 Spadina Road, Toronto, Ontario M5R 2V3
description:
I had a good experience here. I went in for a free hearing test during a promotion years ago.

My hearing tested “above average,” which surprised me because oftentimes my comprehension is so far off and/or delayed when people talk to me. When I mentioned confusion due to noticeable impairment, the staff member conducting my hearing test suggested the issue could be more about how I process sound than how I hear it. I didn’t understand what she meant at the time, but the comment stuck with me over the years.

I have since realized that I experience sensory input (e.g. auditory processing), emotions (e.g. emotional processing), and cognitive (e.g. attention and memory) differences as a neurodivergent (ND) individual. Looking back now, I really appreciate how insightful her comment was.

It was impossible to know the Labour Disruption which would affect our programs.  On May 3, 2025, over 130 participants registered the workshop, "Hearing Loss and Mental Health".  The guest speaker from your organization did not show up without any notice or apology.  Moreover, the unprofessional matters had happened again - two arranged hearing screening clinic days on May 8 and 9 were cancelled without any notice.  I MUST contacted all registered participants individually.  Unbelievable!!! I truly respect the right of the labour disruption.  However, your organization mentioned at the website officially, "We will continue to provide other services on a priority basis.  As always, the interest of our clients and staff are at the heart of all decisions that impact the operation of our organization."  Is it a big joke???

I recently was assessed at CHS referred by a friend. I was astonished at how organized and professional all the staff were. From the admin at the reception offering me a tissue when she noticed my nose running before I did to the overwhelmingly detailed and comprehensive audiology exam with Maria (who was also supervising a university student) the team was polite, empathetic and welcoming. The facility I went to on Spadina was very pleasant and comfortable. As a non-profit there was absolutely no sales emphasis - in fact I was told that aids were not advisable at this time. As an experienced health care worker I have no reservations recommending CHS.

UPDATE:  CHS reached out to me after seeing my review to understand what happened and I am happy with the conversations that occured and the resolution.  I have changed the stars to reflect their response and appreciate that they reached out.

Summary of Original Post: I signed my child up for the Basic Vocab for Youth Course and it was ok but class never ran the full time and there was an excessive amounts of breaks. I then thought give it one more chance with the 101 course and ended up dropping it after the first class as the teacher's set up made it hard to see her and the workbook required a DVD player to be able to do the exercises.  I did not receive acknowledgement from the office when we withdrew from the course and was not impressed with the lack of communication.

Years ago this was a great place to learn ASL. Offered summer immersion courses, had amazing teachers (all were Deaf) and in-person classes. Now only on-line.    Reached out several times to request return to in-person learning since I struggle to learn in a virtual environment. Been going to Bob Rumball for years due to this PLUS I get the impression that the Deaf community is not impressed with CHS. They really dropped the ball.

name: Canadian Hearing Services
address: 1541 Merivale Road, Unit C, Nepean, Ontario K2G 5W1
description:
Very professional and friendly staffs,the best customer service and support!

Extremely professional team right from the booking at the reception to the hearing test with Kate the audiologist. Kate was very helpful with explaining the test results and the next steps. Highly recomended service.

Really sorry no one helps, I wrote a letter a month ago I need help no one helps, sorry no one helps, long wait.

Wonderful teamwork !!! 👍

name: CBI Health (VocationLab)
address: 2 County Court Blvd, Suite 302, Brampton, ON L6W 3W8
description:
I had such a great experience at this physio clinic. My physiotherapist, Rizia, was absolutely amazing. She was knowledgeable, patient, and really took the time to explain everything clearly. I felt genuinely cared for during every session, and she made sure I was comfortable and confident with my treatment plan.

I also want to mention how kind and welcoming the entire staff is. The secretary and everyone at the front desk are very, very kind and always greet you with a smile. It honestly makes such a difference walking into a place that feels so warm and supportive.

Highly recommend this clinic and especially Rizia!

I had a great experience at CBI Health for my physiotherapy. From the moment I walked in, all the staff were friendly, welcoming, and very helpful. They made the whole process smooth and comfortable.
A special thanks to Riza, my physiotherapist, who really took the time to understand my condition and provided treatment that best suited my needs. I truly felt cared for and supported throughout my sessions.
Highly recommend CBI Health to anyone looking for professional and compassionate physiotherapy services.

I had an amazing experience with my physiotherapist Riza Mansuri. . From the very first session, they made me feel comfortable, heard, and genuinely cared for. They took the time to properly assess my condition, explain everything clearly, and create a treatment plan that actually worked for me.

What I appreciate the most is their patience and professionalism. They never rushed appointments and always encouraged me throughout my recovery. I’ve noticed significant improvement in my pain and mobility, and I feel much more confident in my body now.

If you’re looking for someone knowledgeable, kind, and truly dedicated to their patients’ progress, I highly recommend them. I’m really grateful for all the support and guidance I received.

I have been going to physio here for several months now and the staff is very friendly and helpful.  My therapist Riza is very calm, and kind and makes sure that the exercises she gives me do not cause me more pain.  She has taken the time to do things beyond the original scope of my sessions like teach me how to use kinesiology tape in order to alleviate pain in my hip and knee.  She even used my phone to take videos of me doing the assigned exercises so I could watch them at home and replicate the movements.  This is very helpful as I was having trouble remembering all of the exercises she was showing me. This is the third CBI location in Ontario that I have visited and I am extremely pleased with the overall service.

Everyone at CBI Brampton were professional, courteous and a pleasure to deal with. My therapist, Riza, really knew her stuff. Her positive attitude really helped me get over my doubts and concerns about my recovery. I would recommend CBI to anyone who needs physiotherapy.

name: Centre for Immigrant and Community Services (CICS)
address: 2330 Midland Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M1S 5G5
description:
A Welcome Centre that has lots of resources and services. Also has a computer lab, a gym, and a community kitchen.

The lobby is large and has lots of room and there are a lot seating area. There are 3 floors, the 3rd floor is the basement, the 1st floor is the lobby and other rooms area, and the 2nd floor is where most of the employees work at.

The computer lab is on the 2nd floor. The gym is on the basement floor, along with the community kitchen and an area called the "Youth" room.

You could also apply to volunteer here if you so wished to. Volunteers help make CICS run.

Thanks Victoria and Bosco,

I had a lovely and fruitsful gardening season in 2025, I will continue my gardening at home/ balcony.

Keep me update with your wonderful gardening programs!
Especially enjoy your professional workshops and assistance on field whenever I need them in the community garden.

I am at LINC 2/3 in 2019.  The teacher is Stephanie.  I like her teaching method very much.  She is organized, serious, patient, helpful and friendly.  The English she speaks is standard, clear and easy to understand, and she does not say unnecessary things that are not related to the classroom.  It makes you feel that you have learned something without the feeling of wasting time. Stephanie, I'm appreciate you given patient teaching to me.

Such a great place! Grow organic food in gardens or new immigrants. Allows new immigrants to help and learn, non profit.

This community center offers different services to new comers n local. Especially for gardening programs, they run different events n educational services to promote organic vegetables n flowers. How to manage your garden etc etc. I had attended a couple of it n I find it very useful. The staff are very kind n informative.

name: Centre for Skills Development

description:
Been to this place serval times, pretty much everything u got here, u can get from YouTube, and chat GPT. For job search they will send u to warehouse or high turnover labour, if u are male. u refuse, agents will just start ghosting you, except that one time the agent say ur communication skill is lacking and this type of job is the best fit. 🙄
I don’t mind that  if u called urself “center for labor” and be clear about this at get go.🤣

Maybe they can give u something better if u are female. There gona be some reason why low skill customer service jobs are filled with mostly women, hell even security guards. Don’t need me to tell u that many of them don’t even speak English fluently.

I would like to express my gratitude for the exceptional support provided by  Mahira Farazat, professional, welcoming, and attentive to my needs throughout the process.

From the beginning, she took the time to truly listen to my requests and concerns, offering personalized guidance and solutions that made me feel valued and understood. Her willingness to go above and beyond by offering various forms of support has been invaluable in helping me lots.

I have not met anyone with exceptional customer service like that of Leonie.
The kind of approach she has towards clients is of top standard. One minute of chat with her makes you feel that your issue is half way solved.

I had a positive  experience working with Leonie Davis. I felt that she understood what I wanted to achieve and she put my needs first.Overall she made the process so warm and Welcoming I would highly recommend her services.

I had a very positive experience with this organization in Mississauga. Their intake worker, Marwa is very pleasant. And Andalib, who was my counselor, was very knowledgeable, spot on, and she really followed me through the process.
Compared to some other organizations who 'help' you just by emailing you invitations to webinars, Centre for Skills are actually hands on. At least, it seemed to me. And i am thankful to the actual practical support that they gave. Thank you, guys. I'm glad I came across you 🤝

name: Centre Francophone du Grand Toronto
address: 555 Richmond Street West, Suite 303, Toronto, Ontario M5V 3B1
description:
We have been working with Zain for a long time, and it has always been an excellent experience. He consistently referred us high-quality, well-qualified tenants and handled everything with professionalism, care, and a very human approach.

Zain is reliable, attentive, and genuinely focused on creating positive outcomes for everyone involved. His dedication and integrity have made a real difference to our business, and we are very grateful for the collaboration we’ve had with him.

We highly recommend Zain and wish him all the best in this new chapter of his life. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I had a good experience with CFGT by providing excellent support and services to the Francophone community, and the staff are professional, welcoming, and genuinely committed to helping people feel supported and understood. The environment is respectful, inclusive, and well-organized, making it easy to access services and receive guidance.

I would also like to express my sincere appreciation to Zineddine, who was extremely attentive, friendly, and helpful.

Thank you to Jeanne & Barbara too.

I recently had a wonderful experience thanks to the excellent customer service provided by Zinedine. He was very helpful, patient, and genuinely willing to assist. He listened carefully to my concerns and went above and beyond to make sure everything was taken care of. It’s rare to come across someone so dedicated and professional. I truly appreciate his effort and kindness. Highly recommended!

I'll give anytime 5 stars to the Francophone Centre of Fairview.
I am a patient there for more than 10 years. The good thing it starts with parking. You have a free parking at Fairview mall. Very easy access even by subway.
Every time I call for an appointment the Reception/Secretary Brikena always will comfort me with an appointment the first available in the office . I am always happy with the services there by all staff. I am very happy with the doctors, nurses, and the secretary that work at Fairview site.

Centre Francophone Clinique de Fairview—
I’ve been a patient under Dr.Gmora for 10+ years and have always received the best treatment. Unfortunately he is retiring and the new doctor who will replace him along with the receptionist Brikana  are the reason why I left this clinic and found a new doctor.

Brikana the receptionist is extremely rude to patients and lacks basic patient care skills. She yells at patients which is unprofessional and is not always honest about doctor’s availability.

The new doctor, Dr. Yasmine,  needs to learn to listen to patients and know when to outsource when unsure. I came to her complaining of bone pain for a couple weeks, but she kept suggesting that my ACUTE pain was CHRONIC in nature and was insinuating that I had fibromyalgia.

When my lab results would return abnormal, she was never able to provide me a concrete explanation and kept navigating the conversation towards chronic pain even though I had not even met the criteria of chronic pain. My hematology report was abnormal but she didn’t feel the need to refer me to a specialist until I told her my sister’s hematologist took a look and suggested i be referred.

She prescribed me chronic pain medication for pain that was between 7-10/10. After my 2nd emergency visit the doctor prescribed me morphine as he was suspecting a possible sickle cell crisis. She proceeded to say a crisis was impossible for people with sickle cell trait. Clearly she is uneducated as research says it is possible under certain circumstances for people with SCT to experience bone pain/pain crisis. She made me feel crazy for believing the emergency doctor and refused to prescribe me morphine.

When my family stepped in due to her lack of care, and I appointed my sister as the one who would speak for me due to being in so much pain, which I have a right to do, she rudely said to me while in the hospital in pain that she would no longer communicate with my family and accused us of harassing her and the clinic.

Long story short, my pain was CONFIRMED to be related to sickle cell and it was affecting my kidneys which led to 2 back to back hospital admissions. One doctor said I could’ve died if I hadn’t returned to the hospital due to how low my potassium got. If I had stayed under her care and did not have my family “harass” the clinic God knows what would’ve happened to me.

Extremely disappointed in the care I received at Centre Francophone Fairview Clinic from this new doctor.

name: Collège Boréal (Toronto)
address: 34 Distillery Lane, Toronto, Ontario M5A 3C4
description:
ALERT!!! Don't chose this location for TEF canada exam. I had my exam in December. Did it, all done. Was waiting for the results and boom!! Sorry our servers were down so no exam noted. Omg!!! I did notice this issue in the exam day and I DID asked the examiner and ALL HE SAID WAS "NAH LOOK IT's FINE" mind you we were just 14 people giving exam. And for Lord's sake they are asking for another payment before I give the 2nd attempt. Irony is no body is taking accountability. Mind you they aren't trained or guided to solve any such situation which I can guess through their emails. I gave my time and money and this unfortunate situation happened but DON'T DON'T DON'T choose this location. Coming from a work permit holder with limited timeline. I had to leave country few hours after the exam and couldn't find anything else or to even give the exams on the dates offered.I feel hopeless from this management. I thought of not leaving any review here but I can't let this thing happen to anyone else. 410$ fee. I barely earn this in a week. Also the time I had invested to prepare the exam. This is awful and disheartening, specially when the head office mentions me that this location is an independent contractor... oops can't do anything. If something happens to you or your grading or anything. Nobody's gonna take accountability. They aren't trained to conduct exams or maintain their computers or servers. Mind you again, I did pointed the server issue to the examiner at that time and they said its fine let it reload every time and continue. Rest assured I will definitely come see the agent for this centre but I'm so anxious about their reactions in-person. In the end of the day I'm a weaker person but time will tell. As it always does.
I just hope somebody else saves their time and money. Specially when it's coming with the screenshots attached.

The Adapted LINC program at Collège Boréal helped me improve my English and confidence quickly. The teachers were supportive and professional. I’m very grateful for the experience.

I traveled from Vancouver to Toronto and spent a significant amount of money specifically to take the TEF exam at this college. Unfortunately, the experience was unacceptable. The exam room door was left wide open throughout the compréhension écrite, expression écrite, and compréhension orale sections. People were walking by, walking in, speaking and laughing loudly, making it extremely difficult to concentrate even with the headset on—especially during the listening portion, where focus is crucial and the audio cannot be paused nor replayed, as it's an ongoing timed sequence and each audio is played only a single time.

When I raised this concern afterward, with one of the supervisors, (who by the way also contributed to the talking in the hallway) he told me there was “nothing they could do” and that I “should have said something during the exam”—which would have meant missing even more of the audio and getting an even worse grade. No meaningful support was offered beyond a dismissive “sorry, let me know if there’s anything else I can do.”

I usually score close to 90% on mock exams, but this environment severely affected my performance. I would not recommend taking the TEF here, as they clearly lack basic testing protocols and consideration for candidates.

This was by no means an environment appropriate for an exam. It did not feel like a language proficiency test, but rather a test of how many simultaneous distractions one can endure and still perform at their best.
How is this even allowed?

We had a really bad experience, my wife has applied for FSL course, and in the first session of the class, the teacher told her that the class level is too advanced for her, and that she can not continue with that class.

We asked for a refund, and everytime we get the same answer that they will eventually refund us, It has been two months without a refund.

Service offered in French or English. Friendly staff and free access to employment support. It’s not at street level so you have to know it’s there.

name: Community Living Mississauga (Career Connection)
address: 6695 Millcreek Drive, Unit 1, Mississauga, Ontario L5N 5R8
description:
Community living is an organization that desperately needs a make over. Poor administration, bad communication and low level of transparency in their dealings mark this as an organization that should be revamped from top to bottom. I have made complaint over complaint but nothing gets done. I would love to connect with any legal organization that would help in mandating accountability from this poorly run organization. If I could give this organization a ZERO star I would !!

I so like Communitiving mississauga  I lot.. Job coach.not.lot them.cant.get.pay job one cosh got.pay. Is cherry buffin.Chery.job got.pay.job thank you 😊

If I can give this place a 0 star review I would. Service is too slow and too much miss communication. My employment coordinator offerd me job at uoft. I was not able to reach her back about the job. Very dissatisfied with the service.

Great service from miss Laura Kennedy she helped me for everything I need for my son.
she is very nice and friendly helpful reccomend her

It's  very good place for kids with  special needs  please have a nice day god bless you
