Natasha King exemplifies the best of WCG Employment Ontario. She is approachable, knowledgeable, and genuinely cares about the individuals she assists. I am grateful for her guidance and highly recommend her services.

The "resume creation" at this place is a joke. They copy and paste your resume wholesale into public AI, then take the output without editing it and slap it in a resume template. They don't even look at your original resume and try to work from that, they rely solely on the AI. It's a farce that this place says you are required to use them to continue to receive social assistance, while making you visit in person for every appointment, while feeding your private information into public AI in order to do their job for them.

I would be better off doing all of my resume creation and job searching on my own, because at least I have knowledge of how to build a resume properly and not just rely on AI to do the work for me and keep my private information safe. I would give zero stars if I could for this idiocy.

Ramien and the entire team at Employment Ontario have been incredibly supportive throughout my employment journey. They consistently checked in on my welfare and working conditions and made sure I felt supported every step of the way. Their professionalism, care and genuine concern truly set them apart. I am very grateful for their help

I give Ramein a 5 stars! she helped me from WCG. she helped my work and school. She is an amazing employee! and she works hard, very detailed oriented and does her best to give her best to her work! 5 STARS for Ramein my caseworker and also to WCG. for helping individuals who need help for school and work. Godbless! may WCG continue help more people seeking job and school.

I think they follow the rules do nothing looks busy. I go there for better job program and more than 3 months there is no result at all. Keep giving me call appointment after 3 to 4 weeks and keep rejecting the courses. They cant even satifsy the client and keep you waiting until you stopped going there. Absolutely no idea how thing works. Cant even answer the one question. Its been more than 3 months and they cant even find how better job program works what is course eligibility and keep stating different things in different calls. Never go they cant help you.

name: WoodGreen
address: 815 Danforth Avenue, Toronto, Ontario M4J 1L2
description:
OMG walking into the first floor of their employment agency felt more like entering a mental health hospital, talking to mental people than a place meant to help people find jobs. The staff seemed unprepared and unprofessional, leaving me more confused than supported. It is incredibly frustrating that, despite receiving government salary funding, they are falling short of their core mission. Not only did they fail to offer the services I was expecting, but I left with no clear direction or support and I had to run away from them. I couldn’t figure out how to give zero stars on Google reviews because, honestly, they don’t even deserve one but to leave a review, I had to click on 1 star.

I have been to the place for Canada child benefit application and I have been informed about every detail very patiently by YAN. She was very kind and knowledgeable. She also had me booked as I wasn't available in person, for an on call meeting to update me with more benefits I can be eligible for.
For a place where there's a lot of misleading and misinformation, this place have been a proper guide to me. I'm extremely satisfied with YAN's customer service and empathy.
Thank you so much for your service.

I called just to ask about tax return and the lady that answers the phone is extremely rude Didn’t even let me finish, saying what I had to say before cutting me off I understand that she might not be  aware. Of what Im asking or what I meant but that doesn’t give you an excuse to be rude to people who are calling. It is not my first time that she has done this extremely rude. I don’t know why if you don’t like your job then get a new one. Or simply say nicely and kindly that you do not know how you could help me,  it’s all about customer service. Other than that great experience

Very disappointed with the lack of professionalism. I had a confirmed appointment and traveled 1.5 hours to their office, only to be told it was rescheduled — without any prior email, call, or message. That’s a total of 3 hours wasted, with absolutely no respect shown for other people’s time or commitments.

Would give zero out of 5 stars if possible. No hot water for 18 weeks last year, regularly no heating, no cold water (below 92 degrees) for the past year, and now in a heatwave, no AC for 3 days+ for the entire building. Everyone at Woodgreen wants to be a manager and "in charge" but no one wants to do any of the work that is required to be a landlord and provide minimum legal requirements for tenants.

name: Working Skills Centre
address: 55 Eglinton East, Toronto, Ontario M4P 1G8
description:
This is a wonderful institution with very experienced professionals, and extremely kind teachers. I recommend you to study here. You will never regret it and they will take root in your heart, so much that you will love them forever. They are really like a loving family, very caring. Even if you don't like math, you will love math with their teaching (Formulation in Excel).

I cannot recommed these courses and teachers enough! they made these sessions very engaging and rewarding. the topics were broken down into manageable parts. the teachers were passionate and willing to go an extra mile to ensure understanding. they were supportive and encouraging. Thank you.

Working Skills Centre is one employment agency committed to their objectives, ensuring participants acquire the right skills required to secure a job. The environment is warm and welcoming, the staff are helpful, in effect leaving you with memorable moments, and increase confidence in your skill level.

This SAP, QuickBooks, booking and excel   training was so good and experience to know about Canadian accounting and finance knowledge  it helps  me confidential for finding a job and get some interview I recommended any one that want to continue in finance work this training was so interesting and helps full .

This is the best place for gaining great skills for employment, like becoming a proffesional receptionist. Silva is an amazing facilitator,  very knowledgeable  and skilled. Thank you WCS

name: YMCA of Greater Toronto
address: 4580 Dufferin Street, Suite 200, North York, Ontario M3H 5Y2
description:
Extremely disappointing and unprofessional service.

The so-called “job search support” was nothing more than basic CV editing and sending online applications — something that can easily be done independently. There were no real job opportunities, no employer connections, and no added value.

What was even worse, I waited 1.5 weeks for a scheduled English assessment at the Bloor Street location, only to be told on arrival that I was not eligible for the test. This should have been verified before my appointment was confirmed.

A complete waste of time and very poor organization.

I met with Kalsoom today. She is very kind and helped me a lot. Thank you so much for your support.

Useless. They only have low-skilled jobs available and act like you're supposed to accept the scaps. They don't actually want to help you to get a comparable, decent earning job for which you're qualified for.

Came here today with my husband for him to do his EAsl test which was great. But we came to the employment side after to get some help. We came and was pushed to fill out an Ontario Employment participation registration. Only to find out there is no one there to help you. No counsellor there to help. The appointments are so far away almost a week later. My understanding is that an employment Center should have people there to help you even for walk ins. IVS and learning enrichment do and they are funded aswell. To come to an employment center and not have anyone to help I don’t think that is normal.  I called to do one of there programs while I was there and the lady told me they were all full rushing me off the phone. And then I asked her for October  classes she said Oo okay but I’m busy now send me a text. Very unprofessional the whole employment part.

Don't expect Employment services, it has been one month no one call back me as the black girl claim. Even I had been there, not busy at all.

name: YMCA of Greater Toronto
address: 10610 Bayview Avenue, Unit 12, Richmond Hill, Ontario L4C 3N8
description:
They were very helpful and resourceful. Many thanks to Minoo and a host of others working at this location.

I’m amazed with how kind, useful, effective, comforting and successful YMCA Staff is, specially Case Manager Jordan, He has done such a great job and also inspiring! Highly recommended! Every single person there shine on, helpful and hands to work 100%

To all people who are desperately looking for a job and think they can get help from YMCA, if you think this way, well just don't! You won't get any help from them, they're getting paid to help you with your resume, skill development and job search but all they do is blaming you and accusing you and monitoring you instead of helping you, don't waste your time and energy and stay away from them.

The manager and all the workers at this center do nothing. If you go into the center, it’s always empty because they are inactive and don’t even know the YMCA policies.

Very kind and welcoming place, they will help you find any job!

name: York Construction Academy
address: 155 Champagne Drive, Unit 8a, North York, Ontario M3J 2C6
description:
York Construction Academy is an excellent place for learning and growth. It is perfect for people who are looking to gain construction skills in Canada, start a construction career, or improve their knowledge if they already have experience but lack the theoretical aspects.

This academy is ideal for those looking for solutions, knowledge, opportunities, a new career, or a better understanding of how houses are structured in Canada. One of the best things is that the courses are offered in short periods of time and with multiple schedules, which makes it easy to study according to your availability.

However, what you gain here is much more than just classes. You gain real experience, strong knowledge, and excellent support. Bora provides great guidance, and the instructors Leonardo, Milan, and Steff are outstanding. They truly care about students and provide support beyond expectations.

Both the theory and practical classes are very detailed and valuable for future development in the construction industry. If you ever need to come back to strengthen a specific skill, you have the opportunity to do so.

Thank you, York Construction Academy. This has been a great experience in my life in Canada. In the end, you also build strong networking connections with classmates, which is amazing because I found much more than I expected — not only education, but also friendships and cooperation.

I had a great experience at York Construction Academy.
The in-class instructors Milan and Steve were excellent at explaining and demonstrating hands-on skills, including framing, basic electrical, drywall, trim installation, flooring, basic plumbing, window and door installation, painting, and demolition. They did a great job breaking down real construction methods and rough framing, along with all the key elements of the building envelope in a way that was practical and easy to understand.
The theory instructor Nardo was outstanding. He taught the theory behind all of the hands-on work, as well as foundations and blueprint reading. His years of industry experience really showed, and he explained concepts in a way that was easy to retain and directly applicable to real job-site situations.
Overall, the instructors were knowledgeable, professional, and genuinely invested in student success. I would highly recommend this program to anyone looking to build or strengthen a career in construction.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Excellent Construction School With Great Training & Guidance

I had a great experience at this construction school. The instructors give very clear, motivating, and practical lessons that make the concepts easy to understand. Their talks are inspiring, and they take their time to explain everything in a way that even beginners can follow.

The environment is friendly, professional, and focused on helping students build real skills for the construction industry. I learned a lot, gained confidence, and feel more prepared for real-world work.

I highly recommend this school to anyone interested in construction. They truly care about their students and provide quality training that makes a difference.

York Construction Academy provides practical, hands-on training with knowledgeable instructors who are passionate about student success. The learning environment is supportive, well-structured, and focused on real-world construction skills. I highly recommend the academy to anyone looking to build a strong foundation and career in the construction industry.

I recently graduated from York Construction Academy and I can say I made the right decision to choose this college. The staff and the teachers are so nice and friendly. Thanks a lot to Mr Milan , Mr Nardo and Mr Steve. They’re not only wonderful teachers but also good humans with a heart of making sure you understand each and everything. It was such a great experience to learn here :)

name: Youth Employment Services (YES)
address: 1610 Bloor Street West, Toronto, Ontario M6P 1A7
description:
I went to this office for information on Better Jobs Ontario. The orientation went smooth enough. However, speaking to Zarah was a very exhausting experience. She continued stalling and giving conflicting information on whether or not the ministry would pay for my courses. I had to end the call before I lost it.

The next day I went to complain to the manager Diane/Diana & she stated she wouldn`t talk to or take action against Zarah since she was 'doing her job'. I don`t know about you but I heard of people who were just 'doing their job'.

THIS PLACE IS A WASTE. AVOID THIS OFFICE.

***UPDATE 26 FEBRUARY 2026***

I was told by Florencia Boronat that I`d be contacted by the Vice President on the week of 26 Feb. I haven`t heard anything from them all week until I had to email them. I feel that since I had to remind them of a situation they take 'seriously', they said the VP would contact me later that day. I figured they put a 'rush' on trying to get this situation dealt with.

Later the VP (Carrie-Anne Goodfellow) did call me as promised. I was (as expected) given the brush off by her. She said (I`m paraphrasing) 'If you didn`t like our service, please go to another office'. There was no real remedy to resolve the issue at hand which was difficult representatives, conflicting information & managers who refuse to take action. Every time I tried to air my grievances, Carrie-Ann kept trying to interrupt or talk over me. This is behavior unbecoming of a Vice President of any business or charity. When someone needs to vent or air their griefs, the best thing to do is listen more than you talk. I don`t thing Carrie-Ann got that memo. She even went on to say 'We are under funded'. This has nothing to do with the issue at hand. If anything, this is a weak excuse to try to dismiss the situation. She even tried to get off the call while I was complaining. I hope Carrie-Ann`s boss reads this review.

The only good thing (if any) about this is that I complained about 3 other offices and this is the ONLY one that had the professional courtesy to contact me. Still, they did the bare minimum & it`s going to hurt business wise in the long run.

THIS OFFICE IS ONE SAD JOKE.

Awful experience as a job seeker here. I don't know how this place remains in operation. They have to find a way to justify government funding, when in reality they are of little use. I never received proper info on 'start-up cost' assistance which used to be handled by OW, despite repeated requests. This is a reflection of widespread dysfunction across Toronto social services. My worker, Desmond, repeatedly delayed answering emails, (once as long as three weeks!) and always had an excuse. Lack of follow-through for job interviews and no response from management when I e-mailed a complaint. Leaving them was a nightmare too: I had to get an OW supervisor to close my account with them! Stay far away from this place, unless you want to waste your time and be continually frustrated!

with the way phone calls are handled at your office. Unfortunately, the receptionist often does not answer phone calls at all, and when we leave voice messages, no one ever calls back.
Additionally, on the occasions when the phone is answered, the attitude is not polite or professional, which makes the experience even more frustrating.
This lack of responsiveness and professionalism makes communication extremely difficult. I hope this issue will be addressed as soon as possible.

I discovered this place about a month ago and filled out the online form on their website to get services.

However, I didn’t get any response or any acknowledgement that they got my responses from them so I had to go in person to fill out their “application” form.

When I went, the receptionist was nice and acted professional.

Somebody should investigate these folks. Not only they do nothing but they promise stuff like clothes when you get hired and they never deliver on their promises.   Don't waste your time here and if this operates with tax payers money an investigation is due.

name: Youth Services Bureau
address: 1355 Bank Stret, Unit 703, Ottawa, Ontario K1H 8K7
description:
Almost all the bad reviews are from parents who are upset their child isn’t doing EXACTLY as they say. One parents is upset their child if dressing the way they want rather than “the way they’re told to dress” that’s absurd. YSB is a great service and they’ve helped me lots. I’ve been through YSB for several rounds of counselling. Not only that but the walk in too and I’ve had nothing but good experiences. Sometimes it takes some time to find a counsellor you click with but just be patient. YSB is a great support for those suffering from mental health problems. I’ve also had support from Cheo and I would 100% recommend YSB over Cheo unless it’s an emergency situation.

Any time there was a resource there to help me, they fired everyone who made that resource available. The people in charge of this as a business are truly awful people and do not have any people skills or understanding of the real world impacts on individuals who rely on the resource. When I was homeless and needed help with housing, they used to have someone designated to help with phone calls and digging up ads, helping us navigate that. They don't any more.
They also used to have a much wider sexual health care team, and I had so many trans friends who were fired just because they spoke up about issues that mattered. I had long term employees guess my weight openly when registering me as someone who used the service. (I have personal struggles with eating disorders.)

Around this time I also had friends who were volunteers who were doing all the work of full time employees but being paid $20 a week at most. There was significant ableism, transmisogyny, general hostility towards so many of us who never had any options.

Another thing is that they have a policy of not hiring anyone on for pay who has used the resource within the past 3 years. There is absolutely no well-intentioned reason for this rule to be in place. It is inherently a way to make sure that the people who work there never understand what it is like to be a youth relying on the service. And that these youth relying on the service do not get the pathways they need to get out of hard situations. That's not okay.

I have seen far too many people cry because of this business and far too many people overtaxing themselves within an inch of their own lives just because they care about who this organization is supposed to help. Or that they rely on the money involved. But they don't get to say anything about it because the shareholders and business owners hold such a totalitarian grip on it all.

Nothing about us without us is for us.

Very disappointed in this place, my daughter went for counselling to help work through some things. Unfortunately they decided to tell a 15 yr old that she can move out when shes 16 and they'll pay half her rent. My child suffers from anxiety and depression and there answer was for her to move out on her own. Which then caused a ripple effect and she has started losing herself with not wanting to listen, being confrontational not showering or dressing as she was told to dress and do what makes her comfortable. This has caused an issue for school. How far weve come is gone from just 2 counselling apts. I would never refer anyone here if this is there answer to helping with issues. We wanted individual and family counselling but nope sorry I'll find somewhere else to go.

I am shocked at how YSB encourages a teenager ( raised by me since 2 years old and very well adjusted until now ) to leave their primary parent when they 'take their cell phone away' and encourage them to be with the good-time parent with more $ for toys. parent alienation is a real thing and so is the abuse i have been through. The  fact that YSB enables  teenagers to not see the the true light of reality is beyond me. So, if others are considering YSB to help their family, I would suggest another path. It will cost $ but worth it in the end. Please don't count on YSB for anything other than allowing your teenager to take total control and undermine you as a parent. I would like to add that there was a severe, written, breach of my parental privacy here which will have ever lasting effects.

If I could give ZERO, I would.
To think this is where CHEO is sending people?!
I was told that I was the problem, not my child because I told her phone/wifi privileges away when she ran away.
There has to be somewhere else for parents to turn to.

name: South District Office
address: 4035 Meadowbrook Dr, Unit 114, London, ON N6L 1E6
description: Christian Horizons Staff are very helpful and friendly, the have group homes located all over ontario for people with all types of disabilities. They have 24/7 direct personal support workers in place so they can help those who have disabilities with showering, walking, eating, and taking them to appointments. I love there services, and would recomend people to join them.

name: EMPower Employment Program
address: 601 Queens Avenue
description:
Very straightforward and professional. They solely exist to help people who don't have anyone to in their lives to guide them through certain legal processes. Signing in for probation because of a court order, they will sometimes give you the option of calling in. That definitely helps if you're like me or anyone who works full-time.

Thanks again guys your awesome and appreciated.

I need to express my sincere appreciation for all the kindness and support from Brynn, Vogue, and Tracey. I will forever remember how John Howard Society was always helpful and thoughtful and professional.
September 2009 - July 2025.
All the best Tracey 👍🏻

Hannah helped me for over a year. Couldn't be more thankful for JHS.

Excellent staff, very helpful, respectful and knowledgeable. Great guidance for all aspects of life or any issues you might be dealing with. I highly recommend for everyone.

Great organization! This location is small, with a tight parking lot in the back. They suggest parking down off Adelaide.

name: March of Dimes Canada - Paving the Path to Work (Peel Region)
address: 201-2227 South Millway, Mississauga, Ontario L5L 3R6
description:
Very helpful and professional atmosphere.

Very friendly staff

name: London Regional Employment Services
address: 355 Wellington Street
description:
Ive never worked with an employment service before but this was an amazing experience. I worked with Shannon and shes so kind and willing to help through any issues, I got a job and didnt have a photo id and she helped me work out something with my employer and did all the communication. She also helped me be prepped for interviews and know what to say. I also love how you guys help out with uniforms and certificates needed for work.

I recently had the pleasure of working with LEADS, and I couldn't be more impressed with the service I received. My counselor, Kyle Maidens, was absolutely fantastic! He was intuitive, professional, and truly invested in helping me achieve my goals. From the moment I walked in, I felt supported and guided every step of the way. Kyle's expertise and dedication were instrumental in helping me land a job, and I'm forever grateful for his support. If you're looking for a job employment agency that truly cares, look no further than LEADS. Specifically, ask for Kyle Maidens - he's the best!

I want to express my deepest gratitude to Leads Employment Services for their incredible support in my job search. From the very first moment, they showed a very kind and professional attitude. In particular, I want to thank my lead, Erika, who went above and beyond to find the perfect opportunity for me. Erika also helped me formalize my resume, which was essential in achieving my goal. Thanks to her dedication and guidance, I was able to secure a job that perfectly matches my skills and expectations. I highly recommend their services!

I had a wonderful time with LEADs services and was thrilled to work with Tissa during my job search! Tissa helped me refine my resume, guided me through applications, and prepared me for interviews. Her dedication and willingness to support wherever needed made a huge difference in my experience, and I felt more confident throughout the process. I highly recommend LEADs to anyone seeking employment support!

Becky has been super supportive throughout my job search. She always believed in me, kept me motivated, and helped me with interview prep, seminars, and job fairs. She also made sure I was work-ready and had everything I needed for my new job. Thanks to her guidance, I was able to land a great corporate position, and I’m really happy about it! I’m so grateful for all her help and highly recommend her to anyone looking for a caring and professional employment agent.

name: ReStart (John Howard of Kingston and District)
address: 900 Montreal St., Kingston, Ontario K7K 3J9
description:
I want to thank Restart, and especially Julia B for all the hardwork and effort in helping me get a job.
I actually had several job offers, and chose the best forever job!!
Julia went above and beyond my expectations, thanks again!

Barry

Victoria and Peter Young helped me prepare my cover letter and resume. They are highly professional, patient, and truly supportive throughout the process. Their guidance made a big difference, and I felt confident with the final results. I highly recommend their services to anyone seeking career support.

The staff were extremely helpful and friendly, especially Mandie  and Niva. They put me at ease and reassured me. Not only that, but they boosted my confidence! They also enabled me to find a great job. I can't thank them enough for their support. I highly recommend them.

Thankyou Mandie and the supervisor team for helping me get through highschool and upgrading my highschool credits! I'm so appreciative of everyone at restart who has helped me in any way, but Mandie and I have a excellent relationship and Mandie is always willing to help so I'm grateful 🙏 thank you again to everyone at restart for your kind words and lovely resources!!!!!!!I hope many others find their way to this establishment for the amazing greeting and kind words.

So glad I decided to utilise the services offered through restart! Found myself employed so much faster than expected, all thanks to the wonderful and supportive staff at restart. Much thanks to Mrigank, couldn't have asked for a better experience! Thank you so much

name: TNO - The Neighbourhood Organization (Employment Services)
address: 1 Leaside Park Dr., Unit 7
description:
I volunteered here with some work colleagues and had a real appreciation of the volunteers at this food bank. It's very well organized and has a reasonable stock of food items. What I really appreciated was the respect and dignity exhibited towards recipients. It is indeed commendable how this operation is being managed so diligently.  Thanks to all the volunteers that are working at TNO and supporting a noble cause.

In food bank they need front desk some capable person who handle everything without any mistakes.in yesterday I go food bank collect my baby diapers they said I collect them already but I am not collecting yesterday I go first time in this month . When I talked with coordinator she told me they r mistake. It’s not good thing. But front desk people showing attitude they r not mistake anything . Really very disappointing how much incapable person handle front desk

Horrendous Service Experience
I'm writing to express extreme dissatisfaction following what transpired on Saturday July 26th, I sent an email the following day to raise a complaint about the person who I spoke with over the phone almost 2 weeks prior to my visit on July 26th but no one followed up with me as they promised they'll do.

The week of July 14th I completed registration over the phone with an employee named Adeela (that's what the name sounded to me over the phone). She said she had to complete an intake form for me before I could proceed to speak to someone. I clearly explained to her what my needs are in terms of mom-baby support (besides the Early on program) prior to completing registration and she confirmed that I would get that type of help at TNO. When I finished answering all her questions, she mentioned to me that the next step is to book me with a counselor, I told her I can only come on Saturday so she advised July 26th would be good. A counselor will be present to attend to me and provide me with the support I need.

On July 26th I expected a very seamless experience heading to the TNO but I wasn't prepared for the horrendous service experience awaiting me.
I spent the whole morning and part of my afternoon until 2pm trying to find out where I had to go and who would attend to my questions. I ran between the two locations: I went to 10 Gate Blvd and found the office closed and came to 45 Overlea Blvd. I found staff there but I wasn't registered and my name appeared nowhere. Staff on site managed to get me in to speak to a settlement counselor on duty but they said there is nothing they can support with as they are new at TNO and they're specialized in settlement not family services.

All this was happening while I was dragging my 6 month old baby with me as I had no one to watch him and care for him in my absence. Not only my time and efforts were wasted but also baby's time: time to get fed, time to get changed, time to get a nap.

I'm appalled by the indifference, apathy, frustrating, shameful, and disgusting service I witnessed at the hands of your organization which is supposed to do just the opposite: Provide support, genuine understanding, empathy, warmth , generosity and a sense of community.

I requested that someone review my concerns and get back to me soon with a resolution. My concerns are still unresolved to this day, almost  a month after I reported this incident. Why? Because simply no one cares, I'm just a number to help boost their intake data.

Disappointing and Unprofessional Experience with Caseworker

Today I visited TNO Employment Services hoping to apply for Better Jobs Ontario funding. I was assigned to a caseworker named Abdul Hafiz. Unfortunately, my experience was disappointing and felt dismissive from the beginning.

After greeting me, Mr. Hafiz led me to a room and gave me the silent treatment for several minutes. He did not explain the purpose of the meeting or ask any meaningful questions to understand my goals. When he finally spoke, he asked abruptly why I was there — even though I had already explained I was there to apply for Better Jobs Ontario to support my full-time PSW program.

He filled out only the first page of my application, left much of it incomplete, and then told me he would refer me to a settlement worker — someone who is not responsible for the actual funding process. I left confused, unsupported, and feeling like my time was wasted.

As a single parent with two children and a limited window to apply for funding while in school, I expected professionalism, clarity, and support — none of which were provided in this meeting.

I’m now seeking help elsewhere and hope that others won’t face the same experience I did. I
strongly recommend better training and oversight to ensure clients are treated with respect and that critical services like Better Jobs Ontario are handled properly.

— Zeinab Ahmed

The Best Organization !
I had a very good experience there—it’s truly wonderful organization. Nora, Nosheen Khan, Alexandra Bedoev, Maiko Taku, Vijaya Gowri Mohan, Husam(Sam), Mahmoud  Zeineh are the best people. They helped me and gave me a new life. Thanks to all of them! So helpful 🙏🏻🧿✨

name: TNO - The Neighbourhood Organization (Thorncliffe Park)
address: 18 Thorncliffe Park Dr.
description:

On July 26th I expected a very seamless experience heading to the TNO but I wasn't prepared for the horrendous service experience awaiting me.
I spent the whole morning and part of my afternoon until 2pm trying to find out where I had to go and who would attend to my questions. I ran between the two locations: I went to 10 Gate Blvd and found the office closed and came to 45 Overlea Blvd. I found staff there but I wasn't registered and my name appeared nowhere. Staff on site managed to get me in to speak to a settlement counselor on duty but they said there is nothing they can support with as they are new at TNO and they're specialized in settlement not family services.

All this was happening while I was dragging my 6 month old baby with me as I had no one to watch him and care for him in my absence. Not only my time and efforts were wasted but also baby's time: time to get fed, time to get changed, time to get a nap.

I'm appalled by the indifference, apathy, frustrating, shameful, and disgusting service I witnessed at the hands of your organization which is supposed to do just the opposite: Provide support, genuine understanding, empathy, warmth , generosity and a sense of community.

I requested that someone review my concerns and get back to me soon with a resolution. My concerns are still unresolved to this day, almost  a month after I reported this incident. Why? Because simply no one cares, I'm just a number to help boost their intake data.

Disappointing and Unprofessional Experience with Caseworker

Today I visited TNO Employment Services hoping to apply for Better Jobs Ontario funding. I was assigned to a caseworker named Abdul Hafiz. Unfortunately, my experience was disappointing and felt dismissive from the beginning.

After greeting me, Mr. Hafiz led me to a room and gave me the silent treatment for several minutes. He did not explain the purpose of the meeting or ask any meaningful questions to understand my goals. When he finally spoke, he asked abruptly why I was there — even though I had already explained I was there to apply for Better Jobs Ontario to support my full-time PSW program.

He filled out only the first page of my application, left much of it incomplete, and then told me he would refer me to a settlement worker — someone who is not responsible for the actual funding process. I left confused, unsupported, and feeling like my time was wasted.

As a single parent with two children and a limited window to apply for funding while in school, I expected professionalism, clarity, and support — none of which were provided in this meeting.

I’m now seeking help elsewhere and hope that others won’t face the same experience I did. I
strongly recommend better training and oversight to ensure clients are treated with respect and that critical services like Better Jobs Ontario are handled properly.

— Zeinab Ahmed

I recommend Skills for Change and am especially convinced of them because of the workshops by Ms. Sheila Hellier. I have attended three of her workshops so far. Of all the programs I have attended at various Employment Ontario agencies in Toronto, I found hers to be the most comprehensive, in-depth and effective in getting new immigrants up to date on job search strategies, resume making and interview preparation. I especially appreciated the Networking and job search strategies workshop because it gave me a clear picture on job search and a roadmap towards searching for the right job especially since I am looking for switching careers. She is clearly knowledgeable about the topics. There are three aspects of the workshops that impressed me:
1. She is highly knowledgeable and presents these for newcomers in a fresh manner but also in the context of the Canadian job market and workplace. This is very useful for a newcomer. She also has tons of materials which she shares post the workshop for those who ask for it. These are well-curated and very useful to further the application of the topics.
2. She personalises the workshops by starting off with introductions of all the participants and then using that information to relate her workshop topics to each person's  industry and job search requirements. This is deeply helpful because as a job-seeker, it helps us relate to and apply the learning much better.
3. She is a highly energetic, cheerful and humorous lady and presenter. The workshop was never dull, not even for a moment. This was a direly needed relief, given the experience I have had at other such agencies. It convinced me to register with Skills for Change.

I would tell all newcomers to attend these workshops. I believe they are a must-attend for anyone  searching for jobs. Skills for Change should organise more of her workshops and more frequently.

All the other staff here are very welcoming and supportive. It's been a pleasant experience with them. Looking forward to working with them more.

I’m extremely disappointed with my experience with Goodwill’s BJO (Better Jobs Ontario) consultant. I called to explain my situation honestly due to tariffs and slowdown at my workplace, I was only getting 1 shift per week after they open the plant and this was really frustrating how expensive things going up. There was no growth, no hours, and no wage increase for nearly 5 years, only a small $4.50 raise the entire time.

Because of this, I made the decision to leave the job and try to start a new career in the skilled trades. I applied for several apprenticeships and even have proof of applications, but without proper knowledge and training, no employer offered me a position. When I contacted trade schools, they actually referred me to BJO for career retraining support.

But instead of support, I was told that because I quit my job, I must be unemployed for at least 1 year before they can even consider me. This directly contradicts the official BJO eligibility criteria, which clearly states:
•	You may qualify if you’ve been laid off and not working
•	OR if you’re working temporarily just to cover costs
•	OR if you were not laid off but have been unemployed for 12 weeks or more AND are low-income

I’ve been unemployed for over 6 months, actively applying, trying to retrain, and struggling to start a new career — yet I was still refused without proper explanation.

What makes this even more unfair is that one of my former coworkers, with almost the same work history as me, was approved for BJO in another city. Our situations and profiles are almost identical, but the outcome was completely different. This shows a serious lack of consistency, transparency, and fairness in how applications are handled.

She said I have 50 pages of requirements but no real guidance or support. Instead of helping people who are trying to improve their future, they make the process more confusing and discouraging.

I expected professionalism and clarity from a government-funded program, but I left the conversation feeling ignored, judged, and misinformed. Very disappointing and not what BJO promises on their website.

After filling in an application you may qualify for assistance but there is no help if you are at the poverty line, only if you are below it. The building is kept very clean, but not all the workers are friendly. Many have become jaded from years of people abusing the system. Although it can be a hassle, you can get help here if you are struggling to find housing, or finances to feed yourself or your family. The program is not always fair, and not all clients are treated the same. Depending on the worker you are assigned you will be able to get more help easily then others. The system isn't great at this program, and is supported by tax payers, but it is necessary. It would be nice though if they find a way to help their workers from being unfair to clients, and treating everyone equally. They also need to find ways to get more housing in this county. They can provide all the help in the world but if they don't promote the clients to start trying to help themselves then it becomes care that needs to be provided to the client on an ongoing basis. They need to start more work related programs, educational programs,and self care and resource finding programs. These would help their clients become self dependent. This building is well maintained but really that is not what it is about. It is about helping people get and stay financially stable which they do, sort of, when it comes to immediate help, but they don't help very well to set up the client to care for them selves.

They provide guidance, personal support, resume building, networking lessons, skill training, and even financial support! Teaming with them feels like owning a secret-weapon that no other applicants have! Definitely give them a try if you're struggling to find a job!!! A+++

Thank you to Marci for being an amazing, caring and supportive teacher, and for helping me build the confidence for my current job! Also the employment support from Arianna after I graduated the program was amazing! I can confidently say I wouldn't be as successful and confident if it wasn't for the help from these two amazing people!

description:
I had a wonderful experience working with Lisa Ceresney at The Thornhill Employment Hub. She was incredibly supportive and professional throughout my job search process.

Lisa provided me with excellent resources, helped me improve and refine my resume, and guided me step by step until I successfully secured a job. Her knowledge, patience, and genuine willingness to help made a big difference for me.

I truly appreciate her support and highly recommend her services to anyone looking for employment assistance.

I had an appointment with Muina today at 11:00 AM. There was heavy snow, so I checked my email repeatedly all morning to see if any appointments were cancelled, but there was no notice at all.

So, I left home at 9:30 AM, dealt with a major GO Transit delay, and had to pay for two Ubers to make it on time.
When I arrived, surprisingly the center was closed. I waited 20 minutes, called, emailed, rang the doorbell but nothing. So frustrated so disappointed.

Then at 11:45 AM, they called me to say, Sorry, we’re closed today!

That’s not a minor inconvenience that an apology fixes. It’s a clear lack of basic responsibility and respect, especially toward unemployed clients who can hardly afford last-minute transportation costs.

If you’re going to close, notify people in advance. This was careless and unacceptable👎

My consultant, Lisa Ceresney, helped me get a job within just a few weeks of starting after months of unsuccessful searching. She helped me with my resume hands-on and got my dream job quickly. Thanks team!

The Thornhill Employment Hub is a great resource. Valentyna was such a great support for my father. She helped with so much patience and kindness and we truly felt cared of and respected throughout the process. We're so thankful for everything that she has done. The Thornhill Employment Hub is lucky to have her.

If I could put 0 stars I absolutely would. I was told I would need to seek more personal care so they referred me to a therapist which took a whole month and a half, just to charge me, which is an error on their part they said would be fixed. It's an hour till the appointment, it's not fixed, and I am done. I feel manipulated and used by the people who claimed they were here to help me, because I was TOLD I needed several appointments before they could actually help my situation, and then I'm mysteriously charged on my second visit? I'm done. Went here as a kid and a doctor told me I didn't have PTSD because of "needing to have physical trauma" and now I am paying for this severe mistake, and they're barely helping me. Save yourself some tears and wasted time and go somewhere else.

If I could give them something less than one star I would. I went to do my intake that I already did on my phone. Showed up 20 minuets early only to sit 30 minutes past my appointment just to have eyes rolled at me when I asked to reschedule.. you people are rude and don’t care about mental health.. seek the therapy you claim you have there.

UPDATE (04/25) : Here I am, months and months later, to say YIKES. I just had the most unfortunate experience with one of two Psychiatrists who work there. I missed an appointment, due to car issues, which I had called and explained what was going on. They said no problem and rescheduled. Then, later, the refill for my medication was denied. I start to panic, and ended up calling and calling for two weeks before hearing back that it was because I missed my appointment. I explained I was having serious side effects and needed them ASAP. And, when I did get that refill, she acted like she was doing me a favor since it was after a missed appointment. I was dumbfounded. Fast forward to my next appointment, I ask for her to explain the reasoning behind withholding important medications due to missing an appointment, when I still need them regardless of making said appointment, and how irresponsible and dangerous that can be, and as a psychiatrist she should know better. She threw her hands in the air and told me it was MY fault I went without my meds, not hers. It was MY fault because I missed my appointment. Essentially scolding me for not making the appointment. That essentially I didnt deserve medication because I didnt make it to my appointment. This is DANGEROUS. My mental health is tragically reliant on these medications in order for me to function, and survive. She KNOWS this! She is my psychiatrist! She saw the state I was in when I arrived, unmedicated. I was a mess. I could barely talk. And yet, fast forward to now, she thinks she has a right to "punish" me for missing an appointment by withholding my meds and scolding me for missing an appointment, like she's my mother and not my psychiatrist. Truth is she enjoys gatekeeping these important medications, because it makes her feel important. Its sick. I've never been treated so unprofessionally by a "doctor"... Maybe she needs to revisit her education to better understand that what she is doing is dangerous, how she is acting is unprofessional, and people deserve better care than that regardless of income. I hope they review her professional attitude towards patients and take the time to correct some of the behavior I witnessed.

She also made a comment "I see so many people all day every day, I dont have time to check and make sure everyone has their refills!" Likeeeeeeeeeeeeee thats literally your job, ma'am. Also I was asking for them not expecting them without having asked. Seriously. Time to retire.

--------

(2022) It's really unfortunate that low income people don't have better options. Specifically those of us who are trans and/or queer. I tried to have an intake appointment and left within the first five minutes having a panic attack because the old guy apparently hasn't had an sensitivity training in regards to dealing with trans patients. He also insisted I took my mask off which is wildly inappropriate because I am not only immunocompromised but also seeking help due to extreme anxiety. I had to reschedule with someone else, hopefully that goes better, but my initial experience left me feeling hopeless during a time of need.

name: Marion Technical College
address: 1467 Mt. Vernon Ave, Marion, OH 43302
description:
This college as a whole has been amazing. My instructors and the rest of the staff and faculty really care about your education. If I ever have any problems and I go to someone they either know the answer I am looking for or they will find someone who does. If I have any suggestions on how to make things better or easier for students they are always ready and willing to listen what I have to say and make improvements that will better students experiences. I wish I could further my education here farther than an associates degree because I have had such a great experience here. If you are on the fence, sign up! You won’t regret it.

Amazing school. I graduated and walked already after my two years. Every teacher was supportive and there for me. You can just walk in to talk to an advisor (unless during semester start/end). Then it's by appointment. That is so much better than other schools. Osu only has very picky walk in times. MTC teachers put books in the library or I borrowed their copies so I spent nothing. They are friendly and the school is very pretty. Only a couple teachers were annoying, but every place has 1 or 2. I loved my time there.

I have been going to this college 1 year and 9 months so far. I just started the Nursing program. I got put on a wait list last year because the program was full so just a heads up about that. That's not their fault though. My experience with MTC so far has been good. Of course there has been some minor issues, but you'll find that with any college. The staff and teachers have made my experience amazing though! I have loved the support and kind words that almost every single staff member has shown.

After being here for two years I can honestly say that this college is amazingly lit because of the support from the staff, faculty, and the administration. Marion Technical College does a great job giving you the resources (tutoring, resume building and more all for free)  to achieve your goals of complteting your degree. They also help push you to do better and they have personally helped me to get to next. Thank you MTC!!

Absolutely amazing experience! I am in the PTA program and have the most dedicated professors! All of the staff and faculty are very friendly and are invested in my success! Highly recommend this beautiful and uplifting campus!

I contacted Soar Community Services for something as simple as counseling, and the experience was absolutely dreadful from start to finish. After months of waiting, I was bounced around with improper routing, multiple redundant intakes and screenings, and shuffled through counselor after counselor—only to finally have a single meeting with someone new yet again.

The staff seemed poorly trained, communication was nonexistent, and the management is clearly inept. What should have been a straightforward process turned into a long, frustrating ordeal that wasted my time and left me feeling worse, not better.

name: Dunnville Employment Centre
address: 208 Broad St. E., ON N1A 1G2
description:
I have had nothing but wonderful experiences each time I have worked with St. Leonard's! The staff are welcoming and very accommodating. This is a wonderful service to access not only if you are not currently employed but if you are looking to move up in your career. Their interview preparation service is fantastic and allows you to explore your own strengths in a safe and supportive environment. The resume service is extremely professional. We are very fortunate to have all of these services provided free in our small community. A special "shout-out" to Keyana who has been a wonderful support to me in my journey!

Between Sarah and Joanne the support has been incredible. These two truly understand the labor market and the concept of a living wage, something that isn't common in many Employment Ontario affiliates. They are also willing to go above and beyond with their assistance and expertise. I highly recommend them to anyone who is looking for work as they are a wealth of knowledge.

I had the pleasure of working with Mike on a promotion I had been striving for. Mike was amazing in researching my job, my agency and helping me with my interview prep. I was successful! Thank you Mike.

Thanks for your help Mike!  I really appreciate your assistance with everything.  You really went the extra mile!  I would highly recommend St. Leonard's to anyone looking for new employment.

Thank you Bailey and Mike for all your help with my BJO application process. Everybody there is so helpful and friendly, they go above and beyond to help you.

name: The Arc Ontario
address: 3071 County Complex Drive, Canandaigua, NY 14424
description:
ARC is a great place to donate your time and money.  Since not having personal experience with myself or a family member using their services, I was surprised to learn just how many services they offer!!  Every person that I have met that is employed there is so friendly!  As I walk through the halls and see the clients happy activities it truly puts a smile on my face!  Kudos to all involved!!

The Ontario ARC building is a OK size building very confusing to get around if you haven’t been there too often or a lot and the staff can be kind of rude sometimes. And some of them are generally nice too I think overall it is A-OK place in gently really clean and it helps a lot of people in need that have developmental handicaps that need some extra assistance

Have worked with Ontario ARC for about 15 years in conjunction with my son who has severe autism. Over the years, I have had several service coordinators and all have been top notch. Very caring, and seem to really connect with each individual they work with, I've also had excellent experiences with the rec activities OARC puts together for kids AND adults with special needs. I've also had experience with OARC day programs and with community hab staff.

I'm sure like any job where the staff is only able to paid little more than minimum wage, you are liable to find a few slackers or those doing the bare minimum or worse, but I would say at least 95% of my experiences have been overwhelmingly positive ones.

The staff at Ontario ARC go above and beyond for the individuals in their programs every single day! So many unique and fun programs, too! You can see the love and respect for the individuals in all interactions.

Great, knowledgeable people - loved working with them during a training for my three kids with special needs.

name: Publications Ontario
address: 777 Bay Street
description:
A lil late in writing this review, but here I go. This review in particular is for a person named Tracy counter #15. Life had been hard for me for awhile and to encounter her, had made my year! (no joke) She showed such gentleness and kindness which I'll never forget. Without going into too much detail, I needed help with my health card and drivers license. It was a bit complicated and she made the whole process easy and seamless. I'm so very thankful that she was so kind, that I had cried quite a bit behind closed doors. I'm forever grateful to you Tracy! Management please read this, she does her job extremely well and deserves recognition of some sort. I, myself have worked in retail management for over 30 years. So I know excellent customer service when I receive it! Give her an award, raise, praise...something! Hopefully next time I visit, I'll be lucky enough to be sent to you Tracy. Lethargic staff.
Extremely long waiting times.
Irregular ticket call to windows, even for the same service.
No advance appointment booking available at any day/time for over a month.

Just had my 3rd coversation with Service Ontario regarding the issuance of the Green Book for Construction with amendment inserts for complete legislation up to 2022.

1st representative said this publication was available with inserts for $16.00

2nd representative said we can't sell you the amendment inserts with the January 2020 book without having been notified by Occupational Health & Safety that they are available (he said there was not notification within depts that these inserts were ready) so I asked him to put me through to Occupational Health & Safety.

Final representative at Occupational Health & Safety didn't understand what I was asking so we took a bit more time straightening that out which I was happy to do for our mutual benefit.   When she understood what I required, she said the January  2020 edition with amendment inserts was available now fat the cost of $8.00...as I tried to reconcile this half price, she unfortunately displayed an unprofessional manner like I was getting on her last nerve.    I told not to be irritated because I just had to have the correct information (for my report to the prospective purchaser of several copies).   I still don't get why all three people had completely different information.

I don't believe any of them care if we make the purchase with them or not...they still get their pay cheque and their superiors wouldn't have a clue that all three gave me different answers to the same question.   Does anyone have to answer for this apparent pandemonium at Service  Ontario or do the taxpayers (who pay their salary) just have to accept this quality of service?

name: Mind Forward Brain Injury Services
address: 176 Robert Speck Parkway, Mississauga, ON L4Z 3G1
description:
This made a significant difference in my daughter’s life. Before this program, she was at home alone and depressed. Mind forward provides many stimulating and social activities. I would totally recommend that people with a brain injury connect with this organization.

My brother has an acquired brain injury due to a tobogganing accident when we were kids. Over the years he has had several workers all doing their very best to support my brother. The care giver program is a valuable source of information and support. Highly recommend this organization for the person with an acquired brain injury and for the care giver.

Am so  grateful for all these like-minded individuals who care so much. I've been attending this facility for a number of years and I have benefitted so much
Thanks
Oswald Jones

Mind Forward has compassionate staff who care about the clients and focus on the specific needs and care plan of each individual.

They provide case management, day programs and courses, and social drop-in.  They also have an excellent Supported Independent Living (SIL) program for home support for those who can live independently with help.  Case management assists clients by connecting them with important services such as transportation, financial support programs, employment, and counseling.

They are very professional in their approach to care, and very knowledgeable in their field.  They listen to their clients, and act on suggestions for change when they can, in order to improve their services to the community.

The clients they serve all have different needs and are at different levels of functioning both physically and cognitively.   Some clients have visible disabilities, whereas others have invisible disabilities.  Some clients have been in accidents, others are recovering from strokes, and there are those who acquired their brain injuries from medical conditions such as tumors, etc.

A lot of what they do is practical -- they provide classes to help relearn skills such as kitchen prep and different levels of cooking skills, they have active programs like yoga and walking groups, and they offer courses on different types of writing (e.g., creative writing, script writing, poetry).  They also offer music classes for different instruments and levels, vocal, as well as fun groups like karaoke, drama, creative arts, and games groups.

Some additional valuable classes they've offered include memory class to help improve recall and provide additional tools to improve daily functioning, Adulting 101 (home economics and maintenance, meal planning, budgeting,  etc.), managing stress, and courses that provide knowledge about brain injury (including courses on concussion for the community).

During the pandemic, they're using Microsoft Teams to keep clients connected with them and each other, so they don't feel so isolated.  They are running social groups online on different days.

Overall, this is a great organization that has been very helpful and life-changing for many people as they work through brain injury rehab.

Another data point for everyone.  -13c today after the snowstorm yesterday.  4 people in front of me out in the cold when I arrived at 730.  About 30 people in line by 810.  Number handed outdside by 815.  People who come to pick up passports are put in a separate line.  Door open by 822.  We wait on 3rd floor lobby until passport office opened at 830.  All done and out the door by 845.

Make sure you have form filled out and have passport photos before coming.

Had a smooth passport renewal experience. Took an hour and 20 mins total (including coming early to wait in line).

Went on a Monday, arrived at 7:50 AM and there were ~30 people ahead of me.

Security came out at 8:15-8:20 to give everyone tickets to maintain the line order. We went into the building at 8:30-ish and there was some more waiting in a line inside.

After a quick initial check, sat down to wait for my actual passport renewal at 8:40.

Got called up at around 9 and was out by 9:10

I arrived around 11:30am and took about 2 hours, would recommend avoiding lunch time as there’s less workers, the line moved fairly quick after 1pm. Service worker was friendly and helpful. I paid for standard 10 business day + mailing time, however, my passport arrived at my house within 7 days! Pleasantly surprised.

name: St. Clair College — Community Integration Through Cooperative Education (CICE)
address: 2000 Talbot Road West, Windsor, ON, N9A 6S4
description:
As a domestic student who has previously attended other colleges, St Clair College has a wonderful array of features, amenities, and opportunities available to it that many other institutions don't offer.

The protection, security, and investigation program (PSI) has well-informed and kind professors who stress the importance of the career you'll be leading once graduating from the school. They realistically prepare you for what is expected in the field and physically train you.

In terms of extracurriculars... it's really what you make of it. To me, collegiate esports is important. And it is HUGE here. They have an excellent venue, professional arena, and superb gaming lab for all students to use. Scholarship opportunities ($$$) and related program are pretty well managed and worth looking into, if you're a competitive/skilled gamer.

Now... for the complaints. Some of the staff (and certain profs) can be rude and blunt, particularly in the finance and other service areas. This may make it difficult for those who have to deal with international ordeals. Furthermore, it is mildly bothersome when handling online ordeals via school email as some staff and/or profs will leave it unread or not respond at all.

Frankly, your experience with the school will heavily depend on what program you're in due to these behavioral issues and student experience can be hit-or-miss, but for me overall: it's great.

St. Clair College is honestly a disaster. The administration is disorganized, communication is terrible, and the teaching quality is all over the place. On top of that, you’re constantly forced into group projects where half the team doesn’t contribute — so you end up carrying the work while paying thousands in tuition. It feels like a complete waste of time and money.

Unless you’re in a hands-on trade program, most programs feel like you’re just burning cash for a piece of paper. Don’t be fooled by how nice the campus looks — the structure of the studying, class time, and workload is chaotic and poorly managed. For what students pay, this level of organization and academic quality is unacceptable.

Forces students to pay for bus passes even if they have a vehicle or can walk to school. There's a small percentage of students allowed to opt out but once that criteria is met then your student will be forced to pay for the bus pass.

Also have poor scheduling options for full-time employed adults looking to continue education.

Support staff and security do everything in their power to make your day more inconvenient instead of doing things in the best interest of the student they are always looking for a money grab. security does not look like they could safely act in an emergency situation. 6 of them stand around laughing and sit at the desk and roll their eyes in a power trip…newsflash you have very minimal authority,  I wish I could say more positive about this “institution”. Unfortunately Looking for money anywhere they can.

The worst school, paying for nothing. Don’t come over guys. You will regret it. Bus pass is $900 and if you don’t take the buss they still don’t refund it to you. Also professors are not professionals just part time teachers who don’t know how to teach and never learn anything about education just read slides. RUN
